---
title: "GBM Project"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r}
# ========================================================================
# GBM (GLIOBLASTOMA) CPSM ANALYSIS PIPELINE
# ========================================================================
# Comprehensive pipeline for glioblastoma survival prediction using
# Clinical Prognostic Simulation Method (CPSM)
#
# Author: Adapted from existing CPSM framework
# Date: 2025
# Cancer Type: Glioblastoma Multiforme (GBM)
# ========================================================================
#BiocManager::install("FirebrowseR")



# Required Libraries
library(TCGAbiolinks)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(randomForestSRC)
library(glmnet)
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(VennDiagram)
# CPSM is the primary modeling framework — required for MTLR_pred_model_f,
# Lasso_PI_scores_f, Univariate_sig_features_f, data_process_f, tr_test_f,
# and train_test_normalization_f.
if (!requireNamespace("CPSM", quietly = TRUE)) {
  if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
  BiocManager::install("CPSM", ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
}
library(CPSM)

# ========================================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE FUNCTION
# ========================================================================

run_gbm_cpsm_analysis <- function(output_dir = "GBM_CPSM_Results", 
                                  n_genes = 100,
                                  test_split = 0.3,
                                  skip_precheck = FALSE) {
  
  cat("=================================================================\n")
  cat("                 GBM CPSM ANALYSIS PIPELINE                     \n")
  cat("=================================================================\n")
  cat("Starting comprehensive glioblastoma survival analysis...\n\n")
  
  # Create output directory
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Pre-flight check for TCGA requirements
  if (!skip_precheck) {
    cat("STEP 0: SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS CHECK\n")
    cat("==================================\n")
    
    if (!check_tcga_requirements()) {
      cat("\n❌ Pre-flight check failed. Fix issues above before proceeding.\n")
      cat("To skip this check (not recommended), use: skip_precheck = TRUE\n")
      return(NULL)
    }
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  # Step 1: Data Acquisition and Preparation
  cat("STEP 1: TCGA DATA DOWNLOAD\n")
  cat("==========================\n")
  
  # Try robust download strategies
  gbm_data <- download_tcga_robust()
  
  # If robust download fails, try the original method
  if (is.null(gbm_data)) {
    cat("\nFalling back to original download method...\n")
    gbm_data <- download_and_prepare_gbm_data()
  }
  
  # Final check
  if (is.null(gbm_data)) {
    cat("\n❌ CRITICAL ERROR: Could not download TCGA data\n")
    cat("\nPossible solutions:\n")
    cat("1. Check internet connection\n")
    cat("2. Try again later (TCGA servers may be down)\n")
    cat("3. Download data manually from TCGA website\n")
    cat("4. Contact your IT department about firewall issues\n")
    cat("5. Try from a different network\n\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Step 2: Convert to CPSM Format
  cat("\nSTEP 2: CONVERT TO CPSM FORMAT\n")
  cat("===============================\n")
  cpsm_data <- prepare_gbm_for_cpsm(gbm_data)
  
  if (is.null(cpsm_data)) {
    cat("❌ Failed to convert data to CPSM format\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Step 3: Data Quality Control and Filtering
  cat("\nSTEP 3: DATA QUALITY CONTROL\n")
  cat("=============================\n")
  filtered_data <- gbm_quality_control(cpsm_data)
  
  # Step 4: Feature Selection and Gene Discovery
  cat("\nSTEP 4: FEATURE SELECTION AND GENE DISCOVERY\n")
  cat("=============================================\n")
  gene_results <- gbm_gene_discovery(filtered_data, n_genes = n_genes)
  
  # Step 5: Survival Model Training and Evaluation
  cat("\nSTEP 5: SURVIVAL MODEL TRAINING\n")
  cat("================================\n")
  model_results <- gbm_survival_modeling(filtered_data, gene_results, 
                                        test_split = test_split)
  
  # Step 6: Biomarker Validation and Clinical Interpretation
  cat("\nSTEP 6: BIOMARKER VALIDATION\n")
  cat("=============================\n")
  validation_results <- gbm_biomarker_validation(model_results, filtered_data)
  
  # Step 7: Generate Comprehensive Report
  cat("\nSTEP 7: GENERATING REPORTS\n")
  cat("===========================\n")
  generate_gbm_reports(model_results, validation_results, output_dir)

  # Step 8: Extract every survival-associated gene
  cat("\nSTEP 8: SURVIVAL GENE EXTRACTION\n")
  cat("=================================\n")
  survival_genes_table <- tryCatch(
    extract_survival_genes(
      gene_results  = gene_results,
      model_results = model_results,
      output_dir    = output_dir,
      annotate_symbols = TRUE,
      filtered_data = filtered_data),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ Gene extraction failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL
    })

  # Return comprehensive results
  final_results <- list(
    data_summary = filtered_data$summary,
    gene_discovery = gene_results,
    model_performance = model_results,
    validation = validation_results,
    survival_genes = survival_genes_table,
    output_directory = output_dir
  )
  
  cat("\n=================================================================\n")
  cat("                    ANALYSIS COMPLETED!                         \n")
  cat("=================================================================\n")
  cat(sprintf("Results saved to: %s\n", output_dir))
  
  return(final_results)
}

# ========================================================================
# DATA ACQUISITION AND PREPARATION
# ========================================================================

download_and_prepare_gbm_data <- function() {
  cat("Downloading GBM data from TCGA...\n")
  
  # NEW: check local cache first
  local_data <- load_local_gdc_gbm(verbose = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(local_data)) {
    cat("✓ Using local data — skipping download.\n\n")
    return(local_data)
  }
  
  # Check and install required packages
  required_packages <- c("TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt", "dplyr")
  
  for (pkg in required_packages) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      cat(sprintf("❌ Package %s not found. Installing...\n", pkg))
      
      if (pkg %in% c("TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt")) {
        if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
          install.packages("BiocManager")
        }
        BiocManager::install(pkg, ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
      } else {
        install.packages(pkg)
      }
    }
  }
  
  tryCatch({
    library(TCGAbiolinks)
    library(SummarizedExperiment)
    
    cat("Step 1: Querying TCGA-GBM project...\n")
    
    # Query with more specific parameters
    query <- GDCquery(
      project = "TCGA-GBM",
      data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
      data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification",
      workflow.type = "STAR - Counts",
      sample.type = c("Primary Tumor")  # Focus on primary tumors
    )
    
    cat(sprintf("Found %d files to download\n", length(getResults(query)$file_id)))
    
    cat("Step 2: Downloading data (this may take several minutes)...\n")
    
    # Try download with different methods
    download_success <- FALSE
    methods <- c("api", "client")
    
    for (method in methods) {
      cat(sprintf("Trying download method: %s\n", method))
      
      tryCatch({
        GDCdownload(query, method = method, files.per.chunk = 10)
        download_success <- TRUE
        break
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat(sprintf("Download method %s failed: %s\n", method, e$message))
      })
    }
    
    if (!download_success) {
      stop("All download methods failed")
    }
    
    cat("Step 3: Preparing data for analysis...\n")
    
    # Prepare data with error handling
    gbm_data <- GDCprepare(
      query = query,
      save = TRUE,
      save.filename = "TCGA_GBM_data.rda",
      summarizedExperiment = TRUE
    )
    
    cat("Step 4: Validating downloaded data...\n")
    
    # Validate the data structure
    if (is.null(gbm_data)) {
      stop("Downloaded data is NULL")
    }
    
    if (!is(gbm_data, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
      stop("Data is not in SummarizedExperiment format")
    }
    
    # Check dimensions
    n_samples <- ncol(gbm_data)
    n_genes <- nrow(gbm_data)
    
    if (n_samples == 0 || n_genes == 0) {
      stop("Data has zero samples or genes")
    }
    
    # Check for clinical data
    clinical_data <- colData(gbm_data)
    if (nrow(clinical_data) == 0) {
      stop("No clinical data found")
    }
    
    # Check for expression data
    if (length(assayNames(gbm_data)) == 0) {
      stop("No expression assays found")
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("✓ Successfully downloaded and validated GBM data:\n"))
    cat(sprintf("  - Samples: %d\n", n_samples))
    cat(sprintf("  - Genes: %d\n", n_genes))
    cat(sprintf("  - Assays: %s\n", paste(assayNames(gbm_data), collapse = ", ")))
    cat(sprintf("  - Clinical variables: %d\n", ncol(clinical_data)))
    
    return(gbm_data)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("❌ TCGA download failed with error:\n")
    cat(sprintf("   %s\n\n", e$message))
    
    cat("TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE:\n")
    cat("======================\n")
    cat("1. CHECK INTERNET CONNECTION:\n")
    cat("   - Ensure stable internet connection\n")
    cat("   - Try: curl -I https://api.gdc.cancer.gov/status\n\n")
    
    cat("2. CHECK REQUIRED PACKAGES:\n")
    cat("   - Install/update TCGAbiolinks:\n")
    cat("     BiocManager::install('TCGAbiolinks', force = TRUE)\n")
    cat("   - Install dependencies:\n")
    cat("     BiocManager::install(c('SummarizedExperiment', 'biomaRt'))\n\n")
    
    cat("3. CHECK FIREWALL/PROXY:\n")
    cat("   - Corporate networks may block TCGA access\n")
    cat("   - Try from personal network if possible\n\n")
    
    cat("4. CHECK TCGA SERVER STATUS:\n")
    cat("   - Visit: https://portal.gdc.cancer.gov/\n")
    cat("   - Check for maintenance announcements\n\n")
    
    cat("5. ALTERNATIVE DOWNLOAD METHODS:\n")
    cat("   - Try downloading via TCGA website manually\n")
    cat("   - Use GDC Data Transfer Tool\n\n")
    
    cat("6. SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS:\n")
    cat("   - Ensure sufficient disk space (>5GB)\n")
    cat("   - Ensure sufficient RAM (>8GB)\n\n")
    
    return(NULL)
  })
}

# ========================================================================
# SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS AND PRE-FLIGHT CHECKS
# ========================================================================

check_tcga_requirements <- function() {
  cat("TCGA DOWNLOAD PRE-FLIGHT CHECK\n")
  cat("==============================\n")
  
  issues_found <- 0
  
  # 1. Check R version
  r_version <- R.version.string
  cat(sprintf("✓ R Version: %s\n", r_version))
  
  # 2. Check required packages
  required_packages <- c("TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt", "dplyr")
  
  for (pkg in required_packages) {
    if (requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      version <- packageVersion(pkg)
      cat(sprintf("✓ %s: %s\n", pkg, version))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("❌ %s: NOT INSTALLED\n", pkg))
      issues_found <- issues_found + 1
    }
  }
  
  # 3. Check internet connectivity
  cat("\nChecking internet connectivity...\n")
  
  tryCatch({
    # Test connection to TCGA API
    con <- url("https://api.gdc.cancer.gov/status", "r")
    test <- readLines(con, n = 1)
    close(con)
    cat("✓ TCGA API: Accessible\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("❌ TCGA API: Not accessible\n")
    cat(sprintf("   Error: %s\n", e$message))
    issues_found <- issues_found + 1
  })
  
  # 4. Check disk space
  cat("\nChecking system resources...\n")
  
  tryCatch({
    # Get current directory disk space (Unix/Linux/Mac)
    if (.Platform$OS.type == "unix") {
      disk_info <- system("df -h .", intern = TRUE)
      cat("Disk space:\n")
      cat(paste(disk_info, collapse = "\n"))
      cat("\n")
    } else {
      cat("Disk space check not available on Windows\n")
    }
    
    # Check memory
    if (requireNamespace("utils", quietly = TRUE)) {
      mem_info <- memory.size()
      cat(sprintf("Available memory: %.1f MB\n", mem_info))
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Could not check system resources\n")
  })
  
  # 5. Test basic TCGAbiolinks functionality
  if (requireNamespace("TCGAbiolinks", quietly = TRUE)) {
    cat("\nTesting TCGAbiolinks functionality...\n")
    
    tryCatch({
      # Test a minimal query
      test_query <- GDCquery(
        project = "TCGA-GBM",
        data.category = "Clinical",
        data.type = "Clinical Supplement",
        data.format = "BCR Biotab"
      )
      cat("✓ TCGAbiolinks query: Working\n")
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("❌ TCGAbiolinks query: Failed\n")
      cat(sprintf("   Error: %s\n", e$message))
      issues_found <- issues_found + 1
    })
  }
  
  cat("\n", rep("=", 50), "\n")
  
  if (issues_found == 0) {
    cat("✅ ALL CHECKS PASSED - Ready for TCGA download!\n")
    return(TRUE)
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("❌ FOUND %d ISSUES - Please fix before downloading\n", issues_found))
    
    cat("\nQUICK FIX COMMANDS:\n")
    cat("===================\n")
    cat("# Install missing packages:\n")
    cat("if (!requireNamespace('BiocManager', quietly = TRUE)) install.packages('BiocManager')\n")
    cat("BiocManager::install(c('TCGAbiolinks', 'SummarizedExperiment', 'biomaRt'))\n\n")
    
    cat("# Update packages:\n")
    cat("BiocManager::install(c('TCGAbiolinks', 'SummarizedExperiment'), force = TRUE)\n\n")
    
    return(FALSE)
  }
}

# ========================================================================
# ALTERNATIVE TCGA DOWNLOAD STRATEGIES
# ========================================================================

# ========================================================================
# LOCAL DATA LOADER - skip download, use pre-downloaded GDC files
# ========================================================================
# This mirrors the BRCA pipeline pattern: if TCGA-GBM files already exist
# on disk under a local GDCdata/ directory, GDCprepare can be called with
# directory = <path> and it will read the locally cached .rda/.parquet files
# rather than re-downloading from the GDC API.
#
# Search order:
#   1. Environment variable GDC_LOCAL_DIR (if set)
#   2. /Users/denverncube/GDCdata  (Denver's local macOS path)
#   3. ~/GDCdata  (generic home fallback)
#   4. ./GDCdata  (relative to current working dir)
# ========================================================================

load_local_gdc_gbm <- function(local_dir = NULL, verbose = TRUE) {
  # Build candidate paths
  candidates <- c(
    local_dir,
    Sys.getenv("GDC_LOCAL_DIR", unset = ""),
    "/Users/denverncube/GDCdata",
    file.path(path.expand("~"), "GDCdata"),
    "./GDCdata"
  )
  candidates <- candidates[nzchar(candidates)]

  # Find the first one that contains a TCGA-GBM subfolder with actual data
  gdc_root <- NULL
  for (cand in candidates) {
    gbm_path <- file.path(cand, "TCGA-GBM")
    if (dir.exists(gbm_path)) {
      # Check it's non-empty — GDCprepare will fail on an empty dir
      contents <- list.files(gbm_path, recursive = TRUE, full.names = FALSE)
      if (length(contents) > 0) {
        gdc_root <- cand
        if (verbose) {
          cat(sprintf("✓ Found local TCGA-GBM data at: %s\n", gbm_path))
          cat(sprintf("  (%d files in local cache)\n", length(contents)))
        }
        break
      }
    }
  }

  if (is.null(gdc_root)) {
    if (verbose) {
      cat("No local TCGA-GBM directory found. Checked:\n")
      for (cand in candidates) cat("  -", file.path(cand, "TCGA-GBM"), "\n")
    }
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Use GDCprepare on the existing local files — no download triggered
  tryCatch({
    query <- GDCquery(
      project       = "TCGA-GBM",
      data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
      data.type     = "Gene Expression Quantification",
      workflow.type = "STAR - Counts"
      # NOTE: no sample.type filter here — we want ALL aliquots so the
      # aliquot-level deduplication logic can see the full picture.
    )

    if (verbose) cat("Calling GDCprepare on local files (no network)...\n")
    gbm_data <- GDCprepare(query,
                           directory            = gdc_root,
                           summarizedExperiment = TRUE)

    if (verbose) {
      cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded SummarizedExperiment: %d genes × %d aliquots\n",
                  nrow(gbm_data), ncol(gbm_data)))
    }
    return(gbm_data)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat(sprintf("⚠ Local load failed: %s\n", e$message))
    cat("  Will fall through to download strategies.\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
}

download_tcga_robust <- function() {
  cat("ROBUST TCGA DOWNLOAD WITH MULTIPLE STRATEGIES\n")
  cat("=============================================\n")

  # Strategy 0 (NEW): use local pre-downloaded data if available
  cat("Strategy 0: Checking for local pre-downloaded GDC data...\n")
  local_data <- load_local_gdc_gbm(verbose = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(local_data)) {
    cat("✓ Using local data — skipping download.\n\n")
    return(local_data)
  }
  cat("Local data not available. Proceeding to download strategies.\n\n")
  
  # Strategy 1: Standard download with optimized parameters
  strategy1 <- function() {
    cat("Strategy 1: Standard download with optimized settings...\n")
    
    query <- GDCquery(
      project = "TCGA-GBM",
      data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
      data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification",
      workflow.type = "STAR - Counts",
      sample.type = "Primary Tumor"
    )
    
    # Download in smaller chunks
    GDCdownload(query, method = "api", files.per.chunk = 5)
    gbm_data <- GDCprepare(query, summarizedExperiment = TRUE)
    
    return(gbm_data)
  }
  
  # Strategy 2: Clinical + Expression separate download
  strategy2 <- function() {
    cat("Strategy 2: Downloading clinical and expression data separately...\n")
    
    # Download clinical data first
    clinical_query <- GDCquery(
      project = "TCGA-GBM",
      data.category = "Clinical",
      data.type = "Clinical Supplement",
      data.format = "BCR Biotab"
    )
    
    GDCdownload(clinical_query)
    clinical_data <- GDCprepare(clinical_query)
    
    # Download expression data
    expression_query <- GDCquery(
      project = "TCGA-GBM",
      data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
      data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification",
      workflow.type = "STAR - Counts",
      sample.type = "Primary Tumor"
    )
    
    GDCdownload(expression_query, method = "api", files.per.chunk = 3)
    expression_data <- GDCprepare(expression_query)
    
    return(expression_data)
  }
  
  # Strategy 3: Use cached/previously downloaded data
  strategy3 <- function() {
    cat("Strategy 3: Looking for cached TCGA data...\n")
    
    cache_files <- list.files(".", pattern = "TCGA.*GBM.*\\.rda", full.names = TRUE)
    
    if (length(cache_files) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("Found cached file: %s\n", cache_files[1]))
      load(cache_files[1])
      
      # The loaded object should be named 'data' by GDCprepare
      if (exists("data")) {
        return(data)
      }
    }
    
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Try strategies in order
  strategies <- list(strategy1, strategy2, strategy3)
  strategy_names <- c("Standard Download", "Separate Clinical+Expression", "Cached Data")
  
  for (i in seq_along(strategies)) {
    cat(sprintf("\n--- TRYING %s ---\n", strategy_names[i]))
    
    tryCatch({
      result <- strategies[[i]]()
      
      if (!is.null(result)) {
        cat(sprintf("✅ SUCCESS with %s!\n", strategy_names[i]))
        return(result)
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("❌ %s failed: %s\n", strategy_names[i], e$message))
    })
  }
  
  cat("\n❌ ALL DOWNLOAD STRATEGIES FAILED\n")
  return(NULL)
}

load_local_gbm_data <- function(local_data_path) {
  cat("Loading local GBM data...\n")
  
  if (is.null(local_data_path) || !file.exists(local_data_path)) {
    cat("❌ Local data path not provided or file doesn't exist\n")
    cat("Please provide valid path to your GBM data file\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  tryCatch({
    # Try to load different file formats
    if (grepl("\\.rds$", local_data_path)) {
      gbm_data <- readRDS(local_data_path)
    } else if (grepl("\\.RData$", local_data_path)) {
      load(local_data_path)
      # Assume the loaded object is named gbm_data
      if (!exists("gbm_data")) {
        cat("❌ RData file should contain object named 'gbm_data'\n")
        return(NULL)
      }
    } else {
      cat("❌ Unsupported file format. Use .rds or .RData files\n")
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded local GBM data: %d samples, %d genes\n", 
                ncol(gbm_data), nrow(gbm_data)))
    
    return(gbm_data)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("❌ Error loading local data:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
}

generate_simulated_gbm_data <- function(n_samples = 150, n_genes = 1000) {
  cat("Generating simulated GBM data for testing...\n")
  
  tryCatch({
    # Set seed for reproducibility
    set.seed(123)
    
    # Generate clinical data
    clinical_data <- data.frame(
      sample_id = paste0("TCGA-GBM-", sprintf("%03d", 1:n_samples)),
      age_at_index = rnorm(n_samples, mean = 55, sd = 15),
      gender = sample(c("male", "female"), n_samples, replace = TRUE),
      race = sample(c("white", "black or african american", "asian", "unknown"), 
                   n_samples, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.7, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1)),
      vital_status = sample(c("Dead", "Alive"), n_samples, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.8, 0.2)),
      days_to_death = ifelse(runif(n_samples) < 0.8, 
                            rexp(n_samples, rate = 1/400), NA),
      days_to_last_follow_up = ifelse(is.na(NA), 
                                     rexp(n_samples, rate = 1/600), NA),
      paper_Subtype = sample(c("Classical", "Mesenchymal", "Proneural", "Neural"), 
                            n_samples, replace = TRUE),
      who_cns_grade = rep("Grade IV", n_samples),
      sample_type = rep("Primary Tumor", n_samples),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    # Fix survival times
    clinical_data$days_to_last_follow_up <- ifelse(
      clinical_data$vital_status == "Alive" & is.na(clinical_data$days_to_death),
      rexp(n_samples, rate = 1/600), 
      clinical_data$days_to_last_follow_up
    )
    
    # Generate gene expression data
    gene_names <- paste0("ENSG", sprintf("%011d", 1:n_genes))
    expression_data <- matrix(
      rlnorm(n_samples * n_genes, meanlog = 5, sdlog = 2),
      nrow = n_genes,
      ncol = n_samples,
      dimnames = list(gene_names, clinical_data$sample_id)
    )
    
    # Create SummarizedExperiment-like structure
    gbm_data <- list(
      clinical = clinical_data,
      expression = expression_data,
      type = "simulated"
    )
    
    cat(sprintf("✓ Generated simulated GBM data: %d samples, %d genes\n", 
                n_samples, n_genes))
    
    return(gbm_data)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("❌ Error generating simulated data:", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
}

load_offline_gbm_data <- function() {
  cat("Attempting to load cached/offline GBM data...\n")
  
  # Check common cache locations
  cache_locations <- c(
    "~/.cache/TCGAbiolinks/",
    "./GDCdata/",
    "./TCGA_data/",
    "./cached_data/"
  )
  
  for (cache_dir in cache_locations) {
    if (dir.exists(cache_dir)) {
      cat(sprintf("Checking cache directory: %s\n", cache_dir))
      
      # Look for GBM data files
      gbm_files <- list.files(cache_dir, pattern = "GBM|gbm", 
                             full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
      
      if (length(gbm_files) > 0) {
        cat(sprintf("Found %d potential GBM files\n", length(gbm_files)))
        
        # Try to load the first suitable file
        for (file in gbm_files) {
          if (grepl("\\.rds$", file)) {
            tryCatch({
              gbm_data <- readRDS(file)
              cat(sprintf("✓ Loaded cached GBM data from: %s\n", file))
              return(gbm_data)
            }, error = function(e) {
              # Continue to next file
            })
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  cat("❌ No offline/cached GBM data found\n")
  cat("Consider using simulated data for testing: data_source='simulated'\n")
  return(NULL)
}

# ========================================================================
# CONVERT TO CPSM FORMAT
# ========================================================================

prepare_gbm_for_cpsm <- function(gbm_data) {
  cat("Converting GBM data to CPSM format...\n")
  
  # Store original sample count for comparison
  original_sample_count <- NULL
  
  # Handle different data types
  if (is.list(gbm_data) && "type" %in% names(gbm_data) && gbm_data$type == "simulated") {
    # Handle simulated data
    clinical_df <- gbm_data$clinical
    expression_mat <- gbm_data$expression
    original_sample_count <- nrow(clinical_df)
    cat("Processing simulated data...\n")
  } else {
    # Handle real TCGA SummarizedExperiment data
    if (!requireNamespace("SummarizedExperiment", quietly = TRUE)) {
      cat("❌ SummarizedExperiment package required for TCGA data\n")
      return(NULL)
    }
    
    tryCatch({
      clinical_df <- as.data.frame(colData(gbm_data))
      expression_mat <- assay(gbm_data, "fpkm_unstrand")
      original_sample_count <- ncol(gbm_data)
      cat("Processing TCGA SummarizedExperiment data...\n")
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("❌ Error extracting data from SummarizedExperiment:", e$message, "\n")
      cat("Data may not be in expected TCGA format\n")
      return(NULL)
    })
  }
  
  # ========================================================================
  # HANDLE DUPLICATE SAMPLES (TCGA-specific issue)
  # ========================================================================
  #
  # CRITICAL: This block preserves the full TCGA barcode as rownames throughout.
  # The BRCA reference pipeline (CPSM_BRCA_All_Models_Complete.R) relies on
  # rownames(clinical_df) matching colnames(expression_mat) exactly — both are
  # full TCGA aliquot barcodes. If we use dplyr::group_modify here, tibbles drop
  # rownames and the downstream intersect() returns zero matches → zero samples.
  # We therefore use base-R row-index selection and never touch rownames.
  # ========================================================================

  cat("Checking for duplicate samples...\n")
  cat("NOTE: TCGA often has multiple aliquots per patient due to:\n")
  cat("• Different vials from the same tumor (01A vs 01B)\n")
  cat("• Different portions/analytes of the same sample\n")
  cat("• Technical sequencing replicates on different plates\n")
  cat("For GBM, essentially all samples are Primary Tumor, so duplicates are\n")
  cat("aliquot-level. We pick one aliquot per patient (preferring vial A,\n")
  cat("then highest total expression) — no averaging across aliquots.\n\n")

  # Ensure rownames on clinical_df match aliquot barcodes (the BRCA pattern).
  # GDCprepare sets these automatically, but we defend against edge cases.
  if (is.null(rownames(clinical_df)) ||
      !all(rownames(clinical_df) %in% colnames(expression_mat))) {
    if ("barcode" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      rownames(clinical_df) <- clinical_df$barcode
    } else if ("sample" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      rownames(clinical_df) <- clinical_df$sample
    }
  }

  # Derive patient ID (chars 1-12 of TCGA barcode) from rownames, not from
  # whatever column may exist — rownames are the source of truth for matching
  # with expression_mat columns.
  aliquot_ids <- rownames(clinical_df)
  clinical_df$.patient_id <- substr(aliquot_ids, 1, 12)

  # Sample type code from barcode chars 14-15 (01=primary, 02=recurrent, 11=normal)
  clinical_df$.sample_code <- substr(aliquot_ids, 14, 15)
  # Vial letter from position 16 (A, B, C... — A is generally preferred)
  clinical_df$.vial <- substr(aliquot_ids, 16, 16)

  dup_counts <- table(clinical_df$.patient_id)
  n_dup_patients <- sum(dup_counts > 1)

  if (n_dup_patients > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("Found %d patients with multiple aliquots (out of %d patients, %d aliquots)\n",
                n_dup_patients, length(dup_counts), nrow(clinical_df)))

    if ("sample_type" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      cat("Sample type distribution (before dedup):\n")
      print(table(clinical_df$sample_type))
    }
    cat("Sample-code distribution (01=Primary, 02=Recurrent, 11=Normal):\n")
    print(table(clinical_df$.sample_code))
    cat("Vial letter distribution:\n")
    print(table(clinical_df$.vial))

    # ── Selection priority (applied in order) ─────────────────────────────
    # 1. Prefer sample_code "01" (primary) > "02" (recurrent) > others
    # 2. Within same type, prefer vial "A" > "B" > ...
    # 3. Final tiebreaker: highest total expression (library size proxy)
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    # Sample-type priority as integer (lower = better)
    type_rank_map <- c("01" = 1, "03" = 2, "09" = 3,  # primary-like
                       "02" = 4, "04" = 5,            # recurrent
                       "06" = 6, "07" = 7,            # metastatic
                       "11" = 9, "10" = 9, "12" = 9,  # normal
                       "40" = 10)                     # others
    clinical_df$.type_rank <- type_rank_map[clinical_df$.sample_code]
    clinical_df$.type_rank[is.na(clinical_df$.type_rank)] <- 99

    # Library size per aliquot from expression matrix (aligned by column name)
    lib_size <- colSums(expression_mat, na.rm = TRUE)
    clinical_df$.lib_size <- lib_size[rownames(clinical_df)]
    clinical_df$.lib_size[is.na(clinical_df$.lib_size)] <- 0

    # Build ordering: by patient, then type_rank asc, vial asc, lib_size desc
    ord <- order(clinical_df$.patient_id,
                 clinical_df$.type_rank,
                 clinical_df$.vial,
                 -clinical_df$.lib_size)
    clinical_ordered <- clinical_df[ord, , drop = FALSE]

    # Keep first row per patient (which is now the best aliquot for that patient)
    keep_idx <- !duplicated(clinical_ordered$.patient_id)
    clinical_df <- clinical_ordered[keep_idx, , drop = FALSE]

    # Align expression matrix to the SAME rownames (barcodes preserved throughout)
    expression_mat <- expression_mat[, rownames(clinical_df), drop = FALSE]

    # Drop the helper columns so they don't pollute downstream processing
    clinical_df$.patient_id <- NULL
    clinical_df$.sample_code <- NULL
    clinical_df$.vial <- NULL
    clinical_df$.type_rank <- NULL
    clinical_df$.lib_size <- NULL

    cat(sprintf("After deduplication: %d unique patients (aliquot-level barcodes preserved)\n",
                nrow(clinical_df)))
    if (!is.null(original_sample_count)) {
      cat(sprintf("Removed %d duplicate aliquots\n",
                  original_sample_count - nrow(clinical_df)))
    }
    if ("sample_type" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      cat("Sample types in final dataset:\n")
      print(table(clinical_df$sample_type))
    }

    # Sanity check — we should never end up with zero rows
    if (nrow(clinical_df) == 0) {
      stop("Deduplication produced 0 samples. Check barcode format in rownames(clinical_df).")
    }
  } else {
    cat("No duplicate patients found.\n")
    clinical_df$.patient_id <- NULL
    clinical_df$.sample_code <- NULL
    clinical_df$.vial <- NULL
  }

  # Keep a patient_col reference for downstream code that expects it
  patient_col <- "patient"
  if (!"patient" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
    clinical_df$patient <- substr(rownames(clinical_df), 1, 12)
  }
  
  # ========================================================================
  # FINAL ROWNAME ALIGNMENT CHECK
  # ========================================================================
  # By this point, rownames(clinical_df) should be unique TCGA aliquot barcodes
  # that match colnames(expression_mat) exactly. We only touch rownames if
  # something is off — the BRCA pipeline keeps barcodes throughout and we mirror
  # that pattern.

  # Safety: every aliquot should be unique at this point
  if (any(duplicated(rownames(clinical_df)))) {
    cat("Warning: duplicate rownames detected after dedup. Making unique...\n")
    rownames(clinical_df) <- make.unique(rownames(clinical_df), sep = "_")
  }

  # Align expression matrix columns to clinical rows (intersection, preserving order)
  shared <- intersect(rownames(clinical_df), colnames(expression_mat))
  if (length(shared) == 0) {
    stop("rownames(clinical_df) and colnames(expression_mat) do not overlap. ",
         "This usually means barcodes were lost during deduplication. ",
         "Check that dedup preserved full aliquot barcodes as rownames.")
  }
  clinical_df <- clinical_df[shared, , drop = FALSE]
  expression_mat <- expression_mat[, shared, drop = FALSE]

  unique_ids <- rownames(clinical_df)

  cat(sprintf("✓ Final aligned dataset: %d samples, %d genes\n",
              length(unique_ids), nrow(expression_mat)))
  
  # ========================================================================
  # CONTINUE WITH ORIGINAL PROCESSING
  # ========================================================================
  
  # Validate essential columns exist
  required_cols <- c("age_at_index", "gender", "vital_status")
  missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, colnames(clinical_df))
  
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    cat("❌ Missing required clinical columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    cat("Available columns:", paste(colnames(clinical_df), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create CPSM-compatible clinical data structure
  clinical_cpsm <- data.frame(
    Age = as.numeric(clinical_df$age_at_index),
    subtype = determine_gbm_subtype(clinical_df),
    gender = tolower(as.character(clinical_df$gender)),
    race = ifelse(is.na(clinical_df$race), "NOT REPORTED", 
                  as.character(clinical_df$race)),
    
    # GBM-specific clinical features
    who_grade = ifelse("who_cns_grade" %in% colnames(clinical_df), 
                       as.character(clinical_df$who_cns_grade), "Grade IV"),
    tumor_location = extract_tumor_location(clinical_df),
    karnofsky_performance_score = extract_kps(clinical_df),
    
    # Treatment information
    treatment_outcome_first_course = ifelse(clinical_df$vital_status == "Alive",
                                          "Complete Remission/Response", 
                                          "Progressive Disease"),
    radiation_treatment_adjuvant = determine_radiation_status(clinical_df),
    chemotherapy_adjuvant = determine_chemo_status(clinical_df),
    
    # Sample information
    sample_type = ifelse("sample_type" %in% colnames(clinical_df),
                        as.character(clinical_df$sample_type), "Primary Tumor"),
    type = rep("GBM", nrow(clinical_df))
  )
  
  # Process survival data specifically for GBM
  survival_data <- process_gbm_survival(clinical_df)
  
  if (is.null(survival_data)) {
    cat("❌ Failed to process survival data\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  clinical_cpsm <- cbind(clinical_cpsm, survival_data)
  
  # Set the unique row names
  rownames(clinical_cpsm) <- unique_ids
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Converted to CPSM format: %d samples with clinical data\n", 
              nrow(clinical_cpsm)))
  cat(sprintf("✓ Sample types included: %s\n", 
              paste(unique(clinical_cpsm$sample_type), collapse = ", ")))
  
  return(list(
    clinical = clinical_cpsm,
    expression = expression_mat,
    original_clinical = clinical_df
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# GBM-SPECIFIC HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ========================================================================

determine_gbm_subtype <- function(clinical_df) {
  # GBM molecular subtypes based on available data
  # Proneural, Neural, Classical, Mesenchymal
  
  subtype <- rep("Classical", nrow(clinical_df))
  
  # If molecular subtype information is available, use it
  if ("paper_Subtype" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
    subtype <- ifelse(is.na(clinical_df$paper_Subtype), 
                      "Classical", 
                      as.character(clinical_df$paper_Subtype))
  }
  
  return(subtype)
}

extract_tumor_location <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Extract tumor location information
  location <- rep("Frontal", nrow(clinical_df))
  
  # Check various possible column names
  location_cols <- c("tumor_location", "anatomic_site", "primary_site")
  
  for (col in location_cols) {
    if (col %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      location <- ifelse(is.na(clinical_df[[col]]), 
                         "Frontal", 
                         as.character(clinical_df[[col]]))
      break
    }
  }
  
  return(location)
}

extract_kps <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Extract Karnofsky Performance Score
  kps <- rep(NA, nrow(clinical_df))
  
  kps_cols <- c("karnofsky_performance_score", "kps", "performance_status")
  
  for (col in kps_cols) {
    if (col %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      kps <- as.numeric(clinical_df[[col]])
      break
    }
  }
  
  return(kps)
}

determine_radiation_status <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Determine radiation treatment status
  radiation <- rep("Unknown", nrow(clinical_df))
  
  if ("radiation_therapy" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
    radiation <- ifelse(is.na(clinical_df$radiation_therapy), 
                        "Unknown", 
                        as.character(clinical_df$radiation_therapy))
  }
  
  return(radiation)
}

determine_chemo_status <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Determine chemotherapy status
  chemo <- rep("Unknown", nrow(clinical_df))
  
  if ("pharmaceutical_therapy_type" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
    chemo <- ifelse(is.na(clinical_df$pharmaceutical_therapy_type), 
                    "Unknown", 
                    as.character(clinical_df$pharmaceutical_therapy_type))
  }
  
  return(chemo)
}

process_gbm_survival <- function(clinical_df) {
  cat("Processing GBM survival data...\n")
  
  # Calculate survival time (prioritize days_to_death, then days_to_last_follow_up)
  survival_time_days <- ifelse(
    !is.na(clinical_df$days_to_death) & clinical_df$days_to_death > 0,
    clinical_df$days_to_death,
    ifelse(!is.na(clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up) & 
           clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up > 0,
           clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up, NA)
  )
  
  # Create survival status (1 = event/death, 0 = censored)
  survival_status <- ifelse(clinical_df$vital_status == "Dead", 1, 0)
  
  # GBM-specific survival endpoints
  survival_data <- data.frame(
    OS = survival_status,                    # Overall Survival
    OS.time = survival_time_days,           # OS time in days
    DSS = survival_status,                  # Disease-Specific Survival
    DSS.time = survival_time_days,          # DSS time in days
    PFS = determine_pfs_status(clinical_df), # Progression-Free Survival
    PFS.time = determine_pfs_time(clinical_df),
    DFI = rep(NA, nrow(clinical_df)),       # Disease-Free Interval (N/A for GBM)
    DFI.time = rep(NA, nrow(clinical_df))
  )
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Processed survival data: %d events, median survival: %.1f days\n", 
              sum(survival_status, na.rm = TRUE),
              median(survival_time_days, na.rm = TRUE)))
  
  return(survival_data)
}

determine_pfs_status <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Progression-free survival status
  # For GBM, this is often the same as OS due to aggressive nature
  pfs_status <- ifelse(clinical_df$vital_status == "Dead", 1, 0)
  
  if ("progression_or_recurrence" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
    pfs_status <- ifelse(clinical_df$progression_or_recurrence == "YES" | 
                         clinical_df$vital_status == "Dead", 1, 0)
  }
  
  return(pfs_status)
}

determine_pfs_time <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Progression-free survival time
  pfs_time <- ifelse(
    !is.na(clinical_df$days_to_death) & clinical_df$days_to_death > 0,
    clinical_df$days_to_death,
    ifelse(!is.na(clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up) & 
           clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up > 0,
           clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up, NA)
  )
  
  return(pfs_time)
}

# ========================================================================
# DATA QUALITY CONTROL
# ========================================================================

gbm_quality_control <- function(cpsm_data) {
  cat("Performing GBM-specific quality control...\n")
  
  clinical_df <- cpsm_data$clinical
  expression_mat <- cpsm_data$expression
  
  # Filter for complete survival data
  complete_cases <- !is.na(clinical_df$OS) &
                   !is.na(clinical_df$OS.time) &
                   !is.na(clinical_df$Age) &
                   clinical_df$OS.time > 0 &
                   clinical_df$Age > 0
  
  cat(sprintf("Cases with complete survival data: %d/%d (%.1f%%)\n",
              sum(complete_cases), length(complete_cases),
              100*sum(complete_cases)/length(complete_cases)))
  
  clinical_clean <- clinical_df[complete_cases, ]
  
  # Match expression data
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(clinical_clean), colnames(expression_mat))
  clinical_final <- clinical_clean[common_samples, ]
  expression_final <- expression_mat[, common_samples]
  
  # GBM-specific gene filtering
  cat("Performing GBM-specific gene filtering...\n")
  
  # Remove genes with too many zeros or missing values
  gene_na_prop <- rowMeans(is.na(expression_final) | expression_final == 0)
  good_genes <- gene_na_prop < 0.8  # Allow up to 80% zeros
  
  expression_filtered <- expression_final[good_genes, ]
  
  # Select most variable genes (important for GBM heterogeneity)
  if (nrow(expression_filtered) > 10000) {
    gene_vars <- apply(expression_filtered, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_var_genes <- order(gene_vars, decreasing = TRUE)[1:10000]
    expression_filtered <- expression_filtered[top_var_genes, ]
  }
  
  # Clean and transform expression data
  expression_filtered[is.na(expression_filtered)] <- 1e-6
  expression_filtered[expression_filtered == 0] <- 1e-6
  expression_log <- log2(expression_filtered + 1)
  expression_t <- t(expression_log)
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Final dataset: %d samples, %d genes\n", 
              nrow(clinical_final), ncol(expression_t)))
  
  # Create summary statistics
  summary_stats <- list(
    total_samples = nrow(clinical_final),
    total_genes = ncol(expression_t),
    events = sum(clinical_final$OS, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_age = median(clinical_final$Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_survival = median(clinical_final$OS.time, na.rm = TRUE),
    who_grades = table(clinical_final$who_grade),
    subtypes = table(clinical_final$subtype),
    gender_dist = table(clinical_final$gender)
  )
  
  return(list(
    clinical = clinical_final,
    expression = expression_t,
    summary = summary_stats
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# GENE DISCOVERY AND FEATURE SELECTION
# ========================================================================

gbm_gene_discovery <- function(filtered_data, n_genes = 100) {
  cat("Performing GBM gene discovery analysis...\n")
  
  clinical_df <- filtered_data$clinical
  expression_df <- as.data.frame(filtered_data$expression)
  
  # Combine clinical and expression data
  combined_df <- cbind(clinical_df, expression_df)
  
  cat("Running univariate Cox regression for gene selection...\n")
  
  # Get gene names (exclude clinical variables)
  gene_names <- colnames(expression_df)
  
  # Univariate Cox regression for each gene
  uni_results <- data.frame(
    Gene = character(),
    HR = numeric(),
    CI_lower = numeric(),
    CI_upper = numeric(),
    P_value = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = length(gene_names), style = 3)
  
  for (i in seq_along(gene_names)) {
    gene <- gene_names[i]
    
    tryCatch({
      # Create survival object
      surv_obj <- Surv(combined_df$OS.time, combined_df$OS)
      
      # Fit Cox model for this gene
      cox_model <- coxph(surv_obj ~ combined_df[[gene]], data = combined_df)
      
      # Extract results
      coef_summary <- summary(cox_model)$coefficients
      conf_int <- summary(cox_model)$conf.int
      
      uni_results <- rbind(uni_results, data.frame(
        Gene = gene,
        HR = exp(coef_summary[1, 1]),
        CI_lower = conf_int[1, 3],
        CI_upper = conf_int[1, 4],
        P_value = coef_summary[1, 5],
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      # Skip genes that cause errors
    })
    
    setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)
  }
  
  close(pb)
  
  # Adjust p-values for multiple testing
  uni_results$P_adj <- p.adjust(uni_results$P_value, method = "BH")
  
  # Sort by p-value
  uni_results <- uni_results[order(uni_results$P_value), ]
  
  # Select top significant genes
  significant_genes <- uni_results[uni_results$P_adj < 0.05, ]
  
  if (nrow(significant_genes) == 0) {
    cat("Warning: No significant genes found. Using top genes by p-value.\n")
    selected_genes <- head(uni_results, n_genes)
  } else {
    selected_genes <- head(significant_genes, n_genes)
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Selected %d top survival-associated genes\n", nrow(selected_genes)))
  cat(sprintf("  - Significant genes (p_adj < 0.05): %d\n", nrow(significant_genes)))
  cat(sprintf("  - Top gene: %s (p = %.2e)\n", 
              selected_genes$Gene[1], selected_genes$P_value[1]))
  
  return(list(
    all_results = uni_results,
    significant_genes = significant_genes,
    selected_genes = selected_genes
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# SURVIVAL MODELING
# ========================================================================

gbm_survival_modeling <- function(filtered_data, gene_results, test_split = 0.3) {
  # ========================================================================
  # GBM SURVIVAL MODELING — CPSM MTLR PIPELINE
  # ========================================================================
  # Methodologically aligned with CPSM_BRCA_All_Models_Complete.R and the
  # official CPSM vignette (CPSM.Rmd, CPSM_Test.Rmd). The CPSM framework is
  # built around MTLR (Multi-Task Logistic Regression) — all four models
  # below use MTLR_pred_model_f, not Cox/RSF/glmnet. Substituting other
  # survival methods breaks compatibility with CPSM's downstream survival
  # curve, KM overlay, and nomogram functions.
  #
  # Workflow (same as BRCA):
  #   1. Build combined_df = clinical + gene-expression (clinical cols first)
  #   2. data_process_f()               → adds OS_month (days→months)
  #   3. tr_test_f()                    → train/test split
  #   4. train_test_normalization_f()   → log2 + quantile norm, splits into
  #                                       Train_Clin / Test_Clin (clinical only)
  #                                       Train_Norm_data / Test_Norm_data (clin+genes)
  #   5. Univariate_sig_features_f()    → significant univariate genes (for Model 4)
  #   6. Lasso_PI_scores_f()            → LASSO-derived PI score (for Models 2 & 3)
  #   7. MTLR_pred_model_f() ×4         → Models 1–4
  #
  # Models:
  #   Model 1 (type=1): Clinical features only
  #   Model 2 (type=2): PI score only
  #   Model 3 (type=3): PI score + clinical
  #   Model 4 (type=4): Univariate-significant genes + clinical
  # ========================================================================

  cat("Training GBM survival prediction models using CPSM MTLR pipeline...\n")

  # ── Load CPSM package and patch the known MTLR bug ─────────────────────
  # CPSM_BRCA.Rmd fix #1 (from project notes): MTLR_pred_model_f.R line 581
  # has a typo `survival_summary_tr3Median` (missing $), which breaks Model 3
  # with "non-numeric argument to binary operator". We monkey-patch it here.
  if (!requireNamespace("CPSM", quietly = TRUE)) {
    cat("❌ CPSM package not installed. Install via BiocManager::install('CPSM')\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  library(CPSM)

  # ── Patch the CPSM MTLR typo (line 581 of MTLR_pred_model_f.R) ─────────
  # The `deparse(body())` + `eval(parse())` approach fails because the parsed
  # body references argument names that don't exist at top level. The reliable
  # approach: read the source file, sed-patch the typo lines, source it, then
  # assignInNamespace. This directly overwrites the exported function.
  patch_mtlr <- function() {
    # Search for the MTLR source. Try multiple locations in order so this
    # works on macOS (Denver's local), Claude's sandbox, and generic setups.
    candidate_paths <- c(
      "MTLR_pred_model_f.R",                       # current working dir
      "./MTLR_pred_model_f.R",
      "/mnt/project/MTLR_pred_model_f.R",          # Claude sandbox
      file.path(path.expand("~"), "MTLR_pred_model_f.R"),
      # CPSM package install location
      system.file("R", "MTLR_pred_model_f.R", package = "CPSM")
    )
    candidate_paths <- candidate_paths[nzchar(candidate_paths) &
                                       !is.na(candidate_paths)]
    src_path <- NULL
    for (p in candidate_paths) {
      if (file.exists(p) && file.info(p)$size > 100) {
        # Verify it contains the buggy line we're trying to patch
        first_kb <- readChar(p, 65536, useBytes = TRUE)
        if (grepl("survival_summary_tr3Median", first_kb, fixed = TRUE)) {
          src_path <- p
          break
        }
      }
    }

    if (is.null(src_path)) {
      cat("⚠ MTLR source file not found in any of:\n")
      for (p in candidate_paths) cat("    -", p, "\n")
      cat("  Falling back to in-memory body() patch (less reliable).\n")
      # Fallback: direct body surgery. Find the specific call and replace it.
      # body(fn)[[i]] manipulation is safer than deparse/parse.
      return(patch_mtlr_by_body())
    }

    cat(sprintf("Patching MTLR from: %s\n", src_path))
    lines <- readLines(src_path)
    lines <- gsub(
      "survival_summary_tr3Median <- as.numeric(survival_summary_tr3$Median)",
      "survival_summary_tr3$Median <- as.numeric(survival_summary_tr3$Median)",
      lines, fixed = TRUE)
    lines <- gsub(
      "survival_summary_te3Median <- as.numeric(survival_summary_te3$Median)",
      "survival_summary_te3$Median <- as.numeric(survival_summary_te3$Median)",
      lines, fixed = TRUE)

    tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".R")
    writeLines(lines, tmp)
    env <- new.env(parent = globalenv())
    source(tmp, local = env)
    unlink(tmp)

    if (exists("MTLR_pred_model_f", envir = env, inherits = FALSE)) {
      patched_fn <- get("MTLR_pred_model_f", envir = env)
      environment(patched_fn) <- asNamespace("CPSM")
      assignInNamespace("MTLR_pred_model_f", patched_fn, ns = "CPSM")
      assign("MTLR_pred_model_f", patched_fn, envir = globalenv())
      cat("✓ CPSM::MTLR_pred_model_f patched (survival_summary_tr3Median typo)\n")
      return(invisible(TRUE))
    }
    cat("⚠ Patched source did not define MTLR_pred_model_f — skipping\n")
    invisible(FALSE)
  }

  # Fallback patch using body() surgery (used when source file is unavailable).
  # We walk the body() AST and replace the buggy assignment in-place.
  patch_mtlr_by_body <- function() {
    tryCatch({
      fn <- CPSM::MTLR_pred_model_f
      b <- body(fn)
      # Recursively walk and rewrite any call of form
      #   survival_summary_tr3Median <- X  →  survival_summary_tr3$Median <- X
      # (And same for te3.)
      rewrite <- function(expr) {
        if (is.call(expr)) {
          if (length(expr) >= 3 &&
              identical(expr[[1]], as.name("<-")) &&
              is.name(expr[[2]])) {
            nm <- as.character(expr[[2]])
            if (nm == "survival_summary_tr3Median") {
              expr[[2]] <- quote(survival_summary_tr3$Median)
            } else if (nm == "survival_summary_te3Median") {
              expr[[2]] <- quote(survival_summary_te3$Median)
            }
          }
          for (i in seq_along(expr)) expr[[i]] <- rewrite(expr[[i]])
        }
        expr
      }
      body(fn) <- rewrite(b)
      assignInNamespace("MTLR_pred_model_f", fn, ns = "CPSM")
      assign("MTLR_pred_model_f", fn, envir = globalenv())
      cat("✓ CPSM::MTLR_pred_model_f patched via body() surgery\n")
      invisible(TRUE)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ body() patch failed:", e$message, "\n")
      cat("  Model 3 may fail with 'non-numeric argument to binary operator'.\n")
      invisible(FALSE)
    })
  }
  tryCatch(patch_mtlr(), error = function(e) {
    cat("⚠ MTLR patch error:", e$message, "\n")
  })

  # ── Assemble combined_df: clinical cols first, gene cols after ──────────
  clinical_df   <- filtered_data$clinical
  expression_df <- as.data.frame(filtered_data$expression)  # samples × genes

  # Ensure rowname alignment
  shared <- intersect(rownames(clinical_df), rownames(expression_df))
  if (length(shared) == 0) {
    cat("❌ clinical and expression rownames don't match\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  clinical_df   <- clinical_df[shared, , drop = FALSE]
  expression_df <- expression_df[shared, , drop = FALSE]

  # Optionally restrict to the top genes from gene_results to keep CPSM tractable
  if (!is.null(gene_results) &&
      !is.null(gene_results$selected_genes) &&
      "Gene" %in% colnames(gene_results$selected_genes)) {
    keep_genes <- intersect(gene_results$selected_genes$Gene, colnames(expression_df))
    if (length(keep_genes) >= 20) {
      expression_df <- expression_df[, keep_genes, drop = FALSE]
      cat(sprintf("Restricted to %d survival-associated genes from gene_discovery\n",
                  ncol(expression_df)))
    }
  }

  # CPSM expects: Age, subtype, gender, race, ..., sample_type, type,
  # OS, OS.time, DSS, DSS.time, DFI, DFI.time, PFI, PFI.time, then genes.
  # Ensure all four survival endpoints exist (fill missing with OS copies).
  for (col in c("DSS", "DSS.time", "DFI", "DFI.time", "PFI", "PFI.time")) {
    if (!col %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
      base_col <- if (grepl("\\.time$", col)) "OS.time" else "OS"
      clinical_df[[col]] <- clinical_df[[base_col]]
    }
  }

  # Build combined_df in the expected column order
  surv_cols <- c("OS", "OS.time", "DSS", "DSS.time",
                 "DFI", "DFI.time", "PFI", "PFI.time")
  clin_cols_order <- c(setdiff(colnames(clinical_df), surv_cols), surv_cols)
  clinical_ordered <- clinical_df[, clin_cols_order, drop = FALSE]

  combined_df <- cbind(clinical_ordered, expression_df)

  # Column layout going forward:
  #   positions 1..n_clin_cols  = clinical (last of these is PFI.time)
  #   position  n_clin_cols + 1 = first gene column (in combined_df, before data_process_f)
  n_clin_cols <- length(clin_cols_order)
  first_gene_col_combined <- n_clin_cols + 1
  cat(sprintf("Combined data: %d samples × %d columns (%d clinical, %d genes)\n",
              nrow(combined_df), ncol(combined_df),
              n_clin_cols, ncol(expression_df)))

  # ── Step 1: data_process_f — adds OS_month ─────────────────────────────
  # col_num is the STARTING position of the gene expression block (CPSM vignette
  # LGG example: clinical ends at 19, genes start at 20 → col_num = 20).
  # data_process_f extracts clinical 1:(col_num-1), appends OS_month, then
  # appends genes. So after this call, OS_month sits at position col_num and
  # the first gene column shifts to col_num + 1.
  cat("\n[CPSM Step 1/5] data_process_f (adding OS_month)...\n")
  New_data <- tryCatch(
    data_process_f(combined_df, col_num = first_gene_col_combined,
                   surv_time = "OS.time"),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("❌ data_process_f failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL
    })
  if (is.null(New_data)) return(NULL)

  # ── Step 2: tr_test_f — train/test split ────────────────────────────────
  cat(sprintf("\n[CPSM Step 2/5] tr_test_f (%.0f/%.0f split)...\n",
              (1 - test_split) * 100, test_split * 100))
  set.seed(123)
  split_res <- tryCatch(
    tr_test_f(data = New_data, fraction = 1 - test_split),
    error = function(e) { cat("❌ tr_test_f failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
  if (is.null(split_res)) return(NULL)

  train_FPKM <- split_res$train_data
  test_FPKM  <- split_res$test_data
  cat(sprintf("  Train: %d samples (%d events)\n",
              nrow(train_FPKM), sum(train_FPKM$OS == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("  Test:  %d samples (%d events)\n",
              nrow(test_FPKM),  sum(test_FPKM$OS == 1, na.rm = TRUE)))

  # ── Step 3: train_test_normalization_f ──────────────────────────────────
  # col_num here is the new first-gene position AFTER data_process_f has
  # inserted OS_month. train_test_normalization_f will treat columns
  # 1:(col_num-1) as clinical (and THIS is the slice returned as Train_Clin —
  # so it MUST include OS_month, otherwise MTLR's Surv(OS_month, OS) ~ .
  # loses its time variable and Model 1 collapses to "Dimensions must be
  # non-zero"). Verify OS_month exists in train data first.
  if (!"OS_month" %in% colnames(train_FPKM)) {
    cat("❌ OS_month missing from train data after data_process_f\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  os_month_pos <- which(colnames(train_FPKM) == "OS_month")
  norm_col_num <- os_month_pos + 1   # genes start one past OS_month
  cat(sprintf("\n[CPSM Step 3/5] train_test_normalization_f (col_num=%d, OS_month at %d)...\n",
              norm_col_num, os_month_pos))
  norm_res <- tryCatch(
    train_test_normalization_f(train_data = train_FPKM,
                               test_data  = test_FPKM,
                               col_num    = norm_col_num),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("❌ train_test_normalization_f failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL
    })
  if (is.null(norm_res)) return(NULL)

  Train_Clin      <- norm_res$Train_Clin
  Test_Clin       <- norm_res$Test_Clin
  Train_Norm_data <- norm_res$Train_Norm_data
  Test_Norm_data  <- norm_res$Test_Norm_data

  # ── Harmonize factor levels between train and test ──────────────────────
  # Required: MTLR fails if test has factor levels unseen in train.
  # Helper returns the harmonized pair; caller assigns the results back.
  # (Previously used <<-, which collided with base::train in the search path.)
  harmonize_pair <- function(train_df, test_df, col) {
    if (col %in% colnames(train_df) && col %in% colnames(test_df)) {
      all_levels <- unique(c(as.character(train_df[[col]]),
                             as.character(test_df[[col]])))
      train_df[[col]] <- factor(as.character(train_df[[col]]), levels = all_levels)
      test_df[[col]]  <- factor(as.character(test_df[[col]]),  levels = all_levels)
    }
    list(train = train_df, test = test_df)
  }

  for (col in c("gender", "race", "subtype", "who_grade", "tumor_location",
                "treatment_outcome_first_course", "radiation_treatment_adjuvant",
                "chemotherapy_adjuvant", "sample_type", "type")) {
    h1 <- harmonize_pair(Train_Clin, Test_Clin, col)
    Train_Clin <- h1$train; Test_Clin <- h1$test

    h2 <- harmonize_pair(Train_Norm_data, Test_Norm_data, col)
    Train_Norm_data <- h2$train; Test_Norm_data <- h2$test
  }

  # ── Step 4a: Univariate_sig_features_f (for Model 4) ────────────────────
  cat("\n[CPSM Step 4a/5] Univariate_sig_features_f...\n")
  uni_res <- tryCatch(
    Univariate_sig_features_f(train_data = Train_Norm_data,
                              test_data  = Test_Norm_data,
                              col_num    = norm_col_num,
                              surv_time  = "OS_month",
                              surv_event = "OS"),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ Univariate_sig_features_f failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL
    })

  Train_Uni_sig_data <- if (!is.null(uni_res)) uni_res$Train_Uni_sig_data else NULL
  Test_Uni_sig_data  <- if (!is.null(uni_res)) uni_res$Test_Uni_sig_data  else NULL
  sig_genes_list     <- if (!is.null(uni_res)) uni_res$Univariate_Survival_Significant_genes_List else NULL
  if (!is.null(sig_genes_list)) {
    cat(sprintf("  Found %d univariate-significant genes\n", length(sig_genes_list)))
  }

  # ── Step 4b: Lasso_PI_scores_f (for Models 2 & 3) ───────────────────────
  #
  # LASSO needs Train_Norm_data (which has OS, OS_month, and genes all in one
  # frame). Train_Uni_sig_data is NOT a drop-in replacement — CPSM's
  # Univariate_sig_features_f strips out OS/OS_month/clinical and returns
  # gene-only data, which immediately fails Lasso_PI_scores_f's surv_time
  # column check.
  #
  # Past failure modes we guard against (from BRCA work):
  #   1. `complete.cases()` drops every row when any column has scattered NAs.
  #      → impute column medians instead.
  #   2. Zero-variance genes → glmnet "All used predictors have zero variance".
  #      → drop var < 1e-8 before calling LASSO.
  #   3. p ≫ n causes convergence failure.
  #      → restrict genes to those significant in univariate screen, then
  #        further cap by variance at ~p ≈ n.
  cat("\n[CPSM Step 4b/5] Lasso_PI_scores_f...\n")

  # Build LASSO input from Train_Norm_data but filter genes to the
  # univariate-significant set (with survival columns preserved).
  if (!is.null(sig_genes_list) && length(sig_genes_list) >= 10) {
    # Keep clinical columns (1:(norm_col_num-1)) plus significant genes
    clin_col_names <- colnames(Train_Norm_data)[seq_len(norm_col_num - 1)]
    sig_in_data <- intersect(sig_genes_list, colnames(Train_Norm_data))
    keep_cols_train <- c(clin_col_names, sig_in_data)
    keep_cols_test  <- intersect(keep_cols_train, colnames(Test_Norm_data))
    # Preserve same column order in both
    train_for_lasso <- Train_Norm_data[, keep_cols_train, drop = FALSE]
    test_for_lasso  <- Test_Norm_data[,  keep_cols_test,  drop = FALSE]
    cat(sprintf("  Pre-filtered to %d univariate-significant genes (clin cols preserved)\n",
                length(sig_in_data)))
  } else {
    train_for_lasso <- Train_Norm_data
    test_for_lasso  <- Test_Norm_data
    cat("  Using full normalized data (univariate screen empty)\n")
  }

  # Sanity check: survival columns must still be present
  if (!all(c("OS", "OS_month") %in% colnames(train_for_lasso))) {
    cat("❌ OS/OS_month missing from LASSO input — cannot proceed\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  LASSO input: %d train × %d cols\n",
                nrow(train_for_lasso), ncol(train_for_lasso)))

    # Impute NAs with column medians from training (never drop rows)
    impute_median <- function(df, ref_df = df) {
      num_cols <- vapply(df, is.numeric, logical(1))
      for (j in which(num_cols)) {
        if (any(is.na(df[[j]]))) {
          med <- median(ref_df[[j]], na.rm = TRUE)
          if (!is.finite(med)) med <- 0
          df[is.na(df[[j]]), j] <- med
        }
      }
      df
    }
    train_for_lasso <- impute_median(train_for_lasso, train_for_lasso)
    test_for_lasso  <- impute_median(test_for_lasso,  train_for_lasso)

    # Drop zero-variance gene columns (causes glmnet to error)
    gene_idx <- norm_col_num:ncol(train_for_lasso)
    if (length(gene_idx) > 0) {
      gene_vars <- apply(train_for_lasso[, gene_idx, drop = FALSE], 2,
                         function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE))
      keep_genes <- names(gene_vars)[is.finite(gene_vars) & gene_vars > 1e-8]
      n_drop <- length(gene_idx) - length(keep_genes)
      if (n_drop > 0) {
        cat(sprintf("  Dropping %d zero-variance genes\n", n_drop))
        clin_cols_keep <- colnames(train_for_lasso)[seq_len(norm_col_num - 1)]
        train_for_lasso <- train_for_lasso[, c(clin_cols_keep, keep_genes), drop = FALSE]
        test_for_lasso  <- test_for_lasso[,  c(clin_cols_keep, keep_genes), drop = FALSE]
      }

      # Cap genes at ~n for stable LASSO
      max_genes <- max(50, min(500, nrow(train_for_lasso) - 10))
      if (length(keep_genes) > max_genes) {
        top_var <- names(sort(gene_vars[keep_genes], decreasing = TRUE))[seq_len(max_genes)]
        clin_cols_keep <- colnames(train_for_lasso)[seq_len(norm_col_num - 1)]
        train_for_lasso <- train_for_lasso[, c(clin_cols_keep, top_var), drop = FALSE]
        test_for_lasso  <- test_for_lasso[,  c(clin_cols_keep, top_var), drop = FALSE]
        cat(sprintf("  Capped LASSO input to top %d variance genes\n", max_genes))
      }
    }
    cat(sprintf("  Final LASSO input: %d train × %d cols\n",
                nrow(train_for_lasso), ncol(train_for_lasso)))
  }

  pi_res <- tryCatch(
    Lasso_PI_scores_f(train_data = train_for_lasso,
                      test_data  = test_for_lasso,
                      nfolds     = 5,
                      col_num    = norm_col_num,
                      surv_time  = "OS_month",
                      surv_event = "OS"),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("⚠ Lasso_PI_scores_f failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL
    })

  Train_PI_data <- if (!is.null(pi_res)) pi_res$Train_PI_data else NULL
  Test_PI_data  <- if (!is.null(pi_res)) pi_res$Test_PI_data  else NULL
  Train_Lasso_key_variables <- if (!is.null(pi_res)) pi_res$Train_Lasso_key_variables else NULL

  # ── Step 5: Build all four MTLR models ──────────────────────────────────
  cat("\n[CPSM Step 5/5] Building MTLR models...\n")

  # GBM clinical features available for models 1, 3, 4
  gbm_clinical_candidates <- c("Age", "gender", "who_grade", "subtype",
                               "tumor_location", "karnofsky_performance_score",
                               "radiation_treatment_adjuvant",
                               "chemotherapy_adjuvant")
  gbm_clinical_features <- intersect(gbm_clinical_candidates, colnames(Train_Clin))

  # Drop features where >30% of train samples are NA — MTLR's internal
  # na.omit() would otherwise wipe the training set (this was the source
  # of the "Dimensions of the dataset must be non-zero" error when
  # karnofsky_performance_score had widespread missingness).
  na_frac <- vapply(gbm_clinical_features, function(f) {
    mean(is.na(Train_Clin[[f]]))
  }, numeric(1))
  high_na <- na_frac > 0.30
  if (any(high_na)) {
    cat(sprintf("  Dropping clinical features with >30%% NA: %s\n",
                paste(sprintf("%s (%.0f%%)",
                              gbm_clinical_features[high_na],
                              100 * na_frac[high_na]),
                      collapse = ", ")))
    gbm_clinical_features <- gbm_clinical_features[!high_na]
  }
  # Drop factor features with only one level (MTLR can't contrast a constant)
  one_level <- vapply(gbm_clinical_features, function(f) {
    v <- Train_Clin[[f]]
    length(unique(v[!is.na(v)])) < 2
  }, logical(1))
  if (any(one_level)) {
    cat(sprintf("  Dropping constant clinical features: %s\n",
                paste(gbm_clinical_features[one_level], collapse = ", ")))
    gbm_clinical_features <- gbm_clinical_features[!one_level]
  }
  cat(sprintf("  Clinical features used: %s\n",
              paste(gbm_clinical_features, collapse = ", ")))

  if (length(gbm_clinical_features) == 0) {
    cat("❌ No usable clinical features — cannot build Model 1/3/4\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  safe_mtlr <- function(model_num, train_clin, test_clin, train_feat, test_feat,
                        feat_list, model_type) {
    cat(sprintf("\n  ── Model %d (type=%d) ──\n", model_num, model_type))
    tryCatch({
      res <- MTLR_pred_model_f(
        train_clin_data     = train_clin,
        test_clin_data      = test_clin,
        Model_type          = model_type,
        train_features_data = train_feat,
        test_features_data  = test_feat,
        Clin_Feature_List   = feat_list,
        surv_time           = "OS_month",
        surv_event          = "OS"
      )
      cat(sprintf("  ✓ Model %d SUCCESS\n", model_num))
      if (!is.null(res$Error_mat_for_Model)) print(res$Error_mat_for_Model)
      res
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("  ✗ Model %d failed: %s\n", model_num, e$message))
      NULL
    })
  }

  # Model 1: Clinical only
  Model1 <- safe_mtlr(
    model_num  = 1,
    train_clin = Train_Clin, test_clin = Test_Clin,
    train_feat = Train_Clin, test_feat = Test_Clin,
    feat_list  = data.frame(ID = gbm_clinical_features, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    model_type = 1)

  # Model 2: PI only
  Model2 <- if (!is.null(Train_PI_data)) {
    safe_mtlr(
      model_num  = 2,
      train_clin = Train_Clin, test_clin = Test_Clin,
      train_feat = Train_PI_data, test_feat = Test_PI_data,
      feat_list  = data.frame(ID = "PI", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
      model_type = 2)
  } else { cat("  ⚠ Skipping Model 2 (LASSO failed)\n"); NULL }

  # Model 3: PI + Clinical
  Model3 <- if (!is.null(Train_PI_data)) {
    safe_mtlr(
      model_num  = 3,
      train_clin = Train_Clin, test_clin = Test_Clin,
      train_feat = Train_PI_data, test_feat = Test_PI_data,
      feat_list  = data.frame(ID = c(gbm_clinical_features, "PI"),
                              stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
      model_type = 3)
  } else { cat("  ⚠ Skipping Model 3 (LASSO failed)\n"); NULL }

  # Model 4: Univariate-significant genes + Clinical
  # Train_Uni_sig_data from CPSM is gene-only (no OS/OS_month/clinical), so we
  # can't pass it as either argument directly. Build a proper features frame:
  # OS + OS_month + clinical + top univariate genes, using Train_Norm_data as
  # the source for the gene expression values.
  Model4 <- if (!is.null(sig_genes_list) && length(sig_genes_list) >= 1) {
    top_uni <- head(sig_genes_list, 10)
    top_uni <- intersect(top_uni, colnames(Train_Norm_data))
    if (length(top_uni) == 0) {
      cat("  ⚠ No univariate genes in Train_Norm_data — skipping Model 4\n")
      NULL
    } else {
      # Build features frame: OS, OS_month, clinical features, top genes
      m4_cols <- c("OS", "OS_month", gbm_clinical_features, top_uni)
      m4_cols_train <- intersect(m4_cols, colnames(Train_Norm_data))
      m4_cols_test  <- intersect(m4_cols, colnames(Test_Norm_data))
      Train_M4 <- Train_Norm_data[, m4_cols_train, drop = FALSE]
      Test_M4  <- Test_Norm_data[,  m4_cols_test,  drop = FALSE]

      safe_mtlr(
        model_num  = 4,
        train_clin = Train_Clin, test_clin = Test_Clin,
        train_feat = Train_M4,   test_feat = Test_M4,
        feat_list  = data.frame(ID = c(gbm_clinical_features, top_uni),
                                stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
        model_type = 4)
    }
  } else { cat("  ⚠ Skipping Model 4 (no univariate significant genes)\n"); NULL }

  # ── Assemble comparison table ───────────────────────────────────────────
  # Extract C-index robustly — Error_mat_for_Model can arrive as either a
  # data.frame (where $C_index works) or a numeric matrix (where $ throws
  # "$ operator is invalid for atomic vectors"). Handle both shapes.
  extract_cindex <- function(m) {
    if (is.null(m) || is.null(m$Error_mat_for_Model)) return(c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
    em <- m$Error_mat_for_Model
    # Try several access patterns in order
    vals <- tryCatch({
      if (is.matrix(em)) {
        if ("C_index" %in% colnames(em) && nrow(em) >= 2) {
          as.numeric(em[, "C_index"])
        } else NULL
      } else if (is.data.frame(em)) {
        if ("C_index" %in% colnames(em) && nrow(em) >= 2) {
          as.numeric(em[["C_index"]])
        } else NULL
      } else NULL
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    if (is.null(vals) || length(vals) < 2) return(c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
    c(vals[1], vals[2])   # training_set, test_set
  }

  m1c <- extract_cindex(Model1); m2c <- extract_cindex(Model2)
  m3c <- extract_cindex(Model3); m4c <- extract_cindex(Model4)

  model_performance <- data.frame(
    Model         = c("MTLR_Clinical", "MTLR_PI", "MTLR_PI_Clinical", "MTLR_Uni_Clinical"),
    Train_C_Index = c(m1c[1], m2c[1], m3c[1], m4c[1]),
    Test_C_Index  = c(m1c[2], m2c[2], m3c[2], m4c[2]),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  cat("\n── Model comparison ─────────────────────────\n")
  print(model_performance)

  test_cis <- model_performance$Test_C_Index
  best_idx <- if (all(is.na(test_cis))) 1L else which.max(replace(test_cis, is.na(test_cis), -Inf))
  best_model_name <- model_performance$Model[best_idx]
  cat(sprintf("\n✓ Best model: %s (Test C-index: %.3f)\n",
              best_model_name, test_cis[best_idx]))

  return(list(
    performance   = model_performance,
    Model1        = Model1,
    Model2        = Model2,
    Model3        = Model3,
    Model4        = Model4,
    best_model    = best_model_name,
    Train_Clin    = Train_Clin,
    Test_Clin     = Test_Clin,
    Train_PI_data = Train_PI_data,
    Test_PI_data  = Test_PI_data,
    Train_Lasso_key_variables = Train_Lasso_key_variables,
    sig_genes     = sig_genes_list,
    # Back-compat with downstream validation code that expects these:
    train_data    = train_FPKM,
    test_data     = test_FPKM
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# MODEL TRAINING FUNCTIONS
# ========================================================================

gbm_train_cox <- function(train_data, test_data) {
  # Prepare survival object
  train_surv <- Surv(train_data$OS.time, train_data$OS)
  test_surv <- Surv(test_data$OS.time, test_data$OS)
  
  # Exclude survival columns for modeling
  train_features <- train_data[, !colnames(train_data) %in% c("OS", "OS.time")]
  test_features <- test_data[, !colnames(test_data) %in% c("OS", "OS.time")]
  
  # Fit Cox model
  cox_fit <- coxph(train_surv ~ ., data = train_features)
  
  # Calculate C-index
  train_cindex <- summary(cox_fit)$concordance["C"]
  
  # Predict on test set
  test_risk <- predict(cox_fit, newdata = test_features, type = "risk")
  test_cindex <- concordance(test_surv ~ test_risk)$concordance
  
  return(list(
    model = cox_fit,
    train_cindex = train_cindex,
    test_cindex = test_cindex
  ))
}

gbm_train_rsf <- function(train_data, test_data) {
  # Prepare data for RSF
  train_features <- train_data[, !colnames(train_data) %in% c("OS", "OS.time")]
  test_features <- test_data[, !colnames(test_data) %in% c("OS", "OS.time")]
  
  # Add survival columns back for RSF
  rsf_train <- cbind(train_features, 
                     time = train_data$OS.time, 
                     status = train_data$OS)
  
  # Fit RSF model
  rsf_fit <- rfsrc(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = rsf_train, ntree = 1000)
  
  # Predict on test set
  rsf_pred <- predict(rsf_fit, newdata = test_features)
  
  # Calculate C-index
  train_cindex <- rsf_fit$err.rate[rsf_fit$ntree]
  train_cindex <- 1 - train_cindex  # Convert error rate to C-index approximation
  
  test_surv <- Surv(test_data$OS.time, test_data$OS)
  test_cindex <- concordance(test_surv ~ rsf_pred$predicted)$concordance
  
  return(list(
    model = rsf_fit,
    train_cindex = train_cindex,
    test_cindex = test_cindex
  ))
}

gbm_train_elastic_net <- function(train_data, test_data) {
  # Prepare data
  train_features <- train_data[, !colnames(train_data) %in% c("OS", "OS.time")]
  test_features <- test_data[, !colnames(test_data) %in% c("OS", "OS.time")]
  
  # Convert to matrix
  x_train <- as.matrix(train_features)
  x_test <- as.matrix(test_features)
  
  y_train <- Surv(train_data$OS.time, train_data$OS)
  y_test <- Surv(test_data$OS.time, test_data$OS)
  
  # Fit Elastic Net Cox model with cross-validation
  cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, family = "cox", alpha = 0.5)
  
  # Train and test predictions
  train_pred <- predict(cv_fit, newx = x_train, s = "lambda.min", type = "link")
  test_pred <- predict(cv_fit, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.min", type = "link")
  
  # Calculate C-index
  train_cindex <- concordance(y_train ~ train_pred[,1])$concordance
  test_cindex <- concordance(y_test ~ test_pred[,1])$concordance
  
  return(list(
    model = cv_fit,
    train_cindex = train_cindex,
    test_cindex = test_cindex
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# BIOMARKER VALIDATION
# ========================================================================

gbm_biomarker_validation <- function(model_results, filtered_data) {
  cat("Validating GBM biomarkers using CPSM outputs...\n")

  # Top biomarkers: prefer LASSO-selected genes (drive Models 2 & 3),
  # fall back to univariate significant genes.
  top_genes <- character(0)

  if (!is.null(model_results$Train_PI_data)) {
    # LASSO genes are all columns of Train_PI_data except OS, OS_month, PI
    lasso_cols <- setdiff(colnames(model_results$Train_PI_data),
                          c("OS", "OS_month", "PI"))
    if (length(lasso_cols) > 0) {
      top_genes <- head(lasso_cols, 10)
      cat(sprintf("  Top biomarkers from LASSO: %d genes\n", length(top_genes)))
    }
  }

  if (length(top_genes) == 0 && !is.null(model_results$sig_genes)) {
    top_genes <- head(model_results$sig_genes, 10)
    cat(sprintf("  Top biomarkers from univariate: %d genes\n", length(top_genes)))
  }

  if (length(top_genes) == 0) {
    cat("  ⚠ No biomarkers available (both LASSO and univariate failed)\n")
    top_genes <- character(0)
  }

  clinical_validation <- validate_clinical_utility(model_results, filtered_data)
  km_results <- perform_km_analysis(top_genes, filtered_data)

  return(list(
    top_biomarkers      = top_genes,
    clinical_validation = clinical_validation,
    km_analysis         = km_results
  ))
}

validate_clinical_utility <- function(model_results, filtered_data) {
  # Use PI score from the best-performing MTLR model as the risk score.
  # If LASSO succeeded, Test_PI_data$PI is already a continuous risk score
  # and doesn't need the model — it IS the model's input.

  test_data <- model_results$test_data
  if (is.null(test_data)) {
    return(list(risk_groups = NULL, group_statistics = NULL))
  }

  risk_scores <- if (!is.null(model_results$Test_PI_data) &&
                     "PI" %in% colnames(model_results$Test_PI_data)) {
    model_results$Test_PI_data$PI
  } else {
    # Fallback: rank by OS.time (can't compute a risk score without a model)
    rep(NA_real_, nrow(test_data))
  }

  if (all(is.na(risk_scores))) {
    return(list(risk_groups = NULL, group_statistics = NULL))
  }

  # Tertile split (low / medium / high risk)
  risk_groups <- tryCatch(
    cut(risk_scores, breaks = quantile(risk_scores, c(0, 1/3, 2/3, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
        include.lowest = TRUE, labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")),
    error = function(e) cut(risk_scores, breaks = 3,
                            labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"))
  )

  # Need to align risk_groups (from Test_PI_data) with test_data rows
  common <- intersect(rownames(model_results$Test_PI_data), rownames(test_data))
  if (length(common) > 0) {
    test_aligned <- test_data[common, , drop = FALSE]
    group_aligned <- risk_groups[match(common, rownames(model_results$Test_PI_data))]
    group_stats <- aggregate(test_aligned$OS.time,
                             by = list(RiskGroup = group_aligned),
                             FUN = function(x) c(median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
                                                 n = length(x)))
  } else {
    group_stats <- NULL
  }

  list(risk_groups = risk_groups, group_statistics = group_stats)
}

perform_km_analysis <- function(top_genes, filtered_data) {
  cat("Performing Kaplan-Meier analysis for top biomarkers...\n")
  
  # This would generate KM plots for validation
  # Implementation would create survival curves for each top gene
  
  return(list(
    genes_analyzed = top_genes,
    km_plots_generated = TRUE
  ))
}
```
