```{r}
               c("Age", "gender", "gbm_subtype"))
    # Build feature list, dropping any single-level factors
    m3_clin_ids <- c("Age", "gbm_subtype", "PI")
    m3_clin_ids <- setdiff(m3_clin_ids, harm3$dropped)
    if (length(m3_clin_ids) < 2) {
      cat("  ⚠️  Model 3 skipped: too few usable clinical features after dropping single-level factors\n")
      stop("insufficient clinical features")
    }
    Key_Clin_PI_list <- data.frame(ID = m3_clin_ids, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    cat("  Model 3 features:", paste(m3_clin_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n")
    res <- MTLR_pred_model_f(
      train_clin_data    = harm3$train,
      test_clin_data     = harm3$test,
      Model_type         = 3,
      train_features_data = Train_PI_data,
      test_features_data  = Test_PI_data,
      Clin_Feature_List  = Key_Clin_PI_list,
      surv_time          = "OS_month",
      surv_event         = "OS",
      nfolds             = 5
    )
    cat("  Model 3 performance:\n")
    print(res$Error_mat_for_Model)
    res
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("  ⚠️  Model 3 failed:", e$message, "\n")
    NULL
  })
}

# ── Model 4: Univariate genes only ──────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n🔹 Model 4: Univariate-significant genes only\n")

# Use top genes by Concordance (the correct column name from Univariate_sig_features_f)
# Prefer up to 20 top genes to avoid overfitting with MTLR
n_top <- min(20, nrow(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L))
if ("Concordance" %in% colnames(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L)) {
  top_gene_df <- Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L[
    order(as.numeric(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L$Concordance), decreasing = TRUE), ]
} else {
  top_gene_df <- Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L[
    order(as.numeric(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L$pvalue)), ]
}
top_genes_4 <- rownames(top_gene_df)[1:n_top]
Key_Uni_list <- data.frame(ID = top_genes_4, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

model_results$Model4 <- tryCatch({
  res <- MTLR_pred_model_f(
    train_clin_data    = Train_Clin,
    test_clin_data     = Test_Clin,
    Model_type         = 4,
    train_features_data = Train_Uni_sig_data,
    test_features_data  = Test_Uni_sig_data,
    Clin_Feature_List  = Key_Uni_list,
    surv_time          = "OS_month",
    surv_event         = "OS",
    nfolds             = 5
  )
  cat("  Model 4 performance:\n")
  print(res$Error_mat_for_Model)
  checkpoint_save(res, "Model4_result")
  res
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("  ⚠️  Model 4 failed:", e$message, "\n")
  checkpoint_load("Model4_result")
})

# ── Model 5: Univariate genes + Clinical ──────────────────────────────────────
# Uses Model_type = 4 in our embedded MTLR_pred_model_f (genes+clinical path).
# The feature list combines clinical IDs + top univariate gene IDs; the function
# selects whichever of those columns are present in the combined train/test data.
cat("\n🔹 Model 5: Univariate genes + Clinical features\n")
Key_Uni_Clin_list <- data.frame(
  ID = c("Age", "gbm_subtype", top_genes_4),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

model_results$Model5 <- tryCatch({
  harm5   <- harmonize_factor_levels(Train_Clin, Test_Clin,
               c("Age", "gender", "gbm_subtype"))
  m5_clin_ids <- setdiff(c("Age", "gbm_subtype"), harm5$dropped)
  Key_Uni_Clin_list <- data.frame(
    ID = c(m5_clin_ids, top_genes_4),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  cat("  Model 5 clinical features:", paste(m5_clin_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  # Merge harmonized clinical columns into the gene feature frames
  clin_cols   <- c("OS", "OS_month", setdiff(c("Age", "gender", "gbm_subtype"),
                                              harm5$dropped))
  tr5_merged  <- cbind(
    harm5$train[, intersect(clin_cols, colnames(harm5$train)), drop = FALSE],
    Train_Uni_sig_data[rownames(harm5$train),
                       setdiff(colnames(Train_Uni_sig_data), clin_cols), drop = FALSE]
  )
  te5_merged  <- cbind(
    harm5$test[, intersect(clin_cols, colnames(harm5$test)), drop = FALSE],
    Test_Uni_sig_data[rownames(harm5$test),
                      setdiff(colnames(Test_Uni_sig_data), clin_cols), drop = FALSE]
  )
  res <- MTLR_pred_model_f(
    train_clin_data     = harm5$train,
    test_clin_data      = harm5$test,
    Model_type          = 4,
    train_features_data = tr5_merged,
    test_features_data  = te5_merged,
    Clin_Feature_List   = Key_Uni_Clin_list,
    surv_time           = "OS_month",
    surv_event          = "OS",
    nfolds              = 5
  )
  cat("  Model 5 performance:\n")
  print(res$Error_mat_for_Model)
  checkpoint_save(res, "Model5_result")
  res
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("  ⚠️  Model 5 failed:", e$message, "\n")
  checkpoint_load("Model5_result")
})

# ── Model performance summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n📊 MODEL COMPARISON SUMMARY\n")
cat("============================\n")

perf_rows <- list()
for (mn in names(model_results)) {
  mod <- model_results[[mn]]
  if (!is.null(mod) && !is.null(mod$Error_mat_for_Model)) {
    em <- mod$Error_mat_for_Model
    # Error_mat_for_Model is a numeric matrix — use [row, col] not $ accessor
    perf_rows[[mn]] <- data.frame(
      Model         = mn,
      Train_C_index = round(em["Training_set", "C_index"], 4),
      Test_C_index  = round(em["Test_set",     "C_index"], 4),
      Train_MAE     = round(em["Training_set", "Mean_MAE"], 2),
      Test_MAE      = round(em["Test_set",     "Mean_MAE"], 2),
      Train_IBS     = round(em["Training_set", "IBS"], 3),
      Test_IBS      = round(em["Test_set",     "IBS"], 3),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
}
model_perf_df <- do.call(rbind, perf_rows)
print(model_perf_df)

write.csv(model_perf_df, "GBM_Model_Performance.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("💾 Model performance saved: GBM_Model_Performance.csv\n")

# Identify best model by test C-index
best_model_name <- model_perf_df$Model[which.max(model_perf_df$Test_C_index)]
best_model      <- model_results[[best_model_name]]
cat(sprintf("\n🏆 Best model: %s  (Test C-index = %.4f)\n",
            best_model_name,
            model_perf_df$Test_C_index[model_perf_df$Model == best_model_name]))
cat("✅ Step 6 complete\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 7: SURVIVAL CURVE PLOTS (surv_curve_plots_f)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n📊 STEP 7: Survival curve plots\n")
cat("=================================\n")

survCurves_data <- best_model$survCurves_data

if (!is.null(survCurves_data) && nrow(survCurves_data) > 0) {
  # Patient IDs are column names of survCurves_data (col 1 = "time_point", skip it)
  patient_cols <- colnames(survCurves_data)
  patient_cols <- patient_cols[patient_cols != "time_point"]
  sample_id    <- patient_cols[1]
  cat(sprintf("  Highlighting sample: %s\n", sample_id))

  surv_plots <- surv_curve_plots_f(
    Surv_curve_data = survCurves_data,
    selected_sample = sample_id,
    font_size       = 11,
    line_size       = 0.4,
    all_line_col    = "grey70",
    highlight_col   = "firebrick"
  )

  png("GBM_Survival_Curves_All.png", width = 1000, height = 600, res = 120)
  print(surv_plots$all_patients_plot +
          labs(title = "TCGA-GBM: Predicted Survival Curves (All Test Patients)",
               subtitle = sprintf("Model: %s | n = %d", best_model_name,
                                  nrow(survCurves_data))))
  dev.off()

  png("GBM_Survival_Curves_Highlighted.png", width = 1000, height = 600, res = 120)
  print(surv_plots$highlighted_patient_plot +
          labs(title = "TCGA-GBM: Survival Curve – Highlighted Patient",
               subtitle = paste("Sample:", sample_id)))
  dev.off()
  cat("📈 Survival curve plots saved\n")
}
cat("✅ Step 7 complete\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 8: MEAN / MEDIAN SURVIVAL BAR PLOTS (mean_median_surv_barplot_f)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n📊 STEP 8: Mean/Median survival bar plots\n")
cat("==========================================\n")

mean_median_data <- best_model$mean_median_survival_time_data

# Defensive: rename "ID" -> "IDs" if checkpoint was saved before this fix
if (!is.null(mean_median_data) && "ID" %in% colnames(mean_median_data) &&
    !"IDs" %in% colnames(mean_median_data)) {
  colnames(mean_median_data)[colnames(mean_median_data) == "ID"] <- "IDs"
}

if (!is.null(mean_median_data)) {
  bar_colors <- c(
    "TRUE.Mean"    = "#E63946",
    "TRUE.Median"  = "#457B9D",
    "FALSE.Mean"   = "#F4A261",
    "FALSE.Median" = "#A8DADC"
  )

  bar_plots <- mean_median_surv_barplot_f(
    surv_mean_med_data = mean_median_data,
    selected_sample    = sample_id,
    font_size          = 9,
    font_color         = "black",
    bar_colors         = bar_colors
  )

  png("GBM_MeanMedian_Survival_All.png", width = 1000, height = 500, res = 120)
  print(bar_plots$mean_med_all_pat +
          labs(title = "TCGA-GBM: Predicted Mean & Median Survival Times"))
  dev.off()

  png("GBM_MeanMedian_Survival_Highlighted.png", width = 1000, height = 500, res = 120)
  print(bar_plots$highlighted_selected_pat +
          labs(title = "TCGA-GBM: Predicted Survival – Highlighted Patient"))
  dev.off()
  cat("📈 Bar plots saved\n")
}
cat("✅ Step 8 complete\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 9: RISK-GROUP PREDICTION (predict_survival_risk_group_f)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n📊 STEP 9: Risk-group prediction (RSF)\n")
cat("========================================\n")

# Use the feature set matching the best model
best_feature_list <- switch(best_model_name,
  "Model1" = Key_Clin_feature_list,
  "Model2" = Key_PI_list,
  "Model3" = Key_Clin_PI_list,
  "Model4" = Key_Uni_list,
  "Model5" = Key_Uni_Clin_list,
  Key_Uni_list
)

best_train_feat <- switch(best_model_name,
  "Model1" = Train_Clin,
  "Model2" = Train_PI_data,
  "Model3" = Train_PI_data,
  "Model4" = Train_Uni_sig_data,
  "Model5" = Train_Uni_sig_data,
  Train_Uni_sig_data
)
best_test_feat <- switch(best_model_name,
  "Model1" = Test_Clin,
  "Model2" = Test_PI_data,
  "Model3" = Test_PI_data,
  "Model4" = Test_Uni_sig_data,
  "Model5" = Test_Uni_sig_data,
  Test_Uni_sig_data
)

Results_Risk <- tryCatch({
  predict_survival_risk_group_f(
    selected_train_data = best_train_feat,
    selected_test_data  = best_test_feat,
    Feature_List        = best_feature_list
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("⚠️  Risk-group prediction failed:", e$message, "\n")
  NULL
})

if (!is.null(Results_Risk)) {
  Train_results <- Results_Risk$Train_results
  Test_results  <- Results_Risk$Test_results
  cat("Risk-group misclassification results:\n")
  print(Results_Risk$misclassification_results)
  cat("Test set risk predictions:\n")
  print(head(Test_results))
}
cat("✅ Step 9 complete\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 10: KM OVERLAY PLOT (km_overlay_plot_f)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n📊 STEP 10: KM overlay plot\n")
cat("============================\n")

if (!is.null(Results_Risk)) {
  KM_plot <- tryCatch({
    km_overlay_plot_f(
      Train_results    = Train_results,
      Test_results     = Test_results,
      survcurve_te_data = survCurves_data,
      selected_sample  = sample_id,
      font_size        = 12,
      train_palette    = c("firebrick", "steelblue"),
      test_curve_col   = "darkgreen",
      test_curve_size  = 1.2,
      test_curve_lty   = "dashed",
      annotation_col   = "black"
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("⚠️  KM overlay failed:", e$message, "\n")
    NULL
  })

  if (!is.null(KM_plot)) {
    png("GBM_KM_Overlay_Plot.png", width = 1000, height = 700, res = 120)
    print(KM_plot)
    dev.off()
    cat("📈 KM overlay plot saved: GBM_KM_Overlay_Plot.png\n")
  }
}
cat("✅ Step 10 complete\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 11: NOMOGRAM (Nomogram_generate_f)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n📊 STEP 11: Nomogram\n")
cat("=====================\n")

# Nomogram uses training data with selected features
# Build nomogram feature list (clinical + top univariate genes)
# Use fewer genes (3 max) to prevent label overlap in the nomogram
n_nomo <- min(3, length(top_genes_4))
nomo_feature_list <- data.frame(
  ID = c("Age", "gbm_subtype",
         top_genes_4[1:n_nomo]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Combine Train_Clin + Train_Uni_sig_data for the nomogram input
nomo_cols <- intersect(nomo_feature_list$ID,
                       colnames(Train_Uni_sig_data))
nomo_train <- cbind(Train_Clin,
                    Train_Uni_sig_data[, setdiff(nomo_cols, colnames(Train_Clin)),
                                       drop = FALSE])

nomo_result <- tryCatch({
  Nomogram_generate_f(
    data         = nomo_train,
    Feature_List = nomo_feature_list,
    surv_time    = "OS_month",
    surv_event   = "OS",
    font_size    = 0.45,
    axis_cex     = 0.35,
    tcl_len      = 0.3,
    label_margin = 1.0,
    col_grid     = gray(c(0.85, 0.95))
  )
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("⚠️  Nomogram failed:", e$message, "\n")
  NULL
})

if (!is.null(nomo_result)) {
  cat("Nomogram C-index:\n")
  print(nomo_result$C_index_mat)
  # Nomogram is rendered to an active device by the function;
  # wrap it to save to PNG — use larger canvas + wider margins to prevent text overlap
  png("GBM_Nomogram.png", width = 1800, height = 1100, res = 120)
  par(mar = c(5, 8, 4, 3))  # extra left margin for long gene/subtype labels
  Nomogram_generate_f(
    data         = nomo_train,
    Feature_List = nomo_feature_list,
    surv_time    = "OS_month",
    surv_event   = "OS",
    font_size    = 0.45,
    axis_cex     = 0.35,
    tcl_len      = 0.3,
    label_margin = 1.0,
    col_grid     = gray(c(0.85, 0.95))
  )
  dev.off()
  cat("📈 Nomogram saved: GBM_Nomogram.png\n")
}
cat("✅ Step 11 complete\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SUPPLEMENTARY PLOTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n🎨 Generating supplementary plots...\n")

# ── Plot A: Volcano plot of univariate genes ─────────────────────────────────
plot_volcano <- function(uni_df, title = "TCGA-GBM: Univariate Survival Genes") {
  # Ensure expected columns exist
  if (!all(c("pvalue", "HR") %in% colnames(uni_df))) {
    cat("⚠️  Volcano: missing 'pvalue' or 'HR' columns – skipping\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  df <- uni_df
  df$Gene    <- if ("Gene_Symbol" %in% colnames(df)) df$Gene_Symbol else rownames(df)
  df$Gene[is.na(df$Gene)] <- rownames(df)[is.na(df$Gene)]
  df$log2HR  <- log2(df$HR)
  df$negLogP <- -log10(df$pvalue)
  df$Direction <- ifelse(df$HR > 1, "High-risk (HR > 1)", "Protective (HR < 1)")
  df$label_gene <- ifelse(df$negLogP > quantile(df$negLogP, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
                          df$Gene, "")

  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2HR, y = negLogP, colour = Direction, label = label_gene)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.8) +
    geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = 15, segment.size = 0.3) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("High-risk (HR > 1)"  = "#E63946",
                                   "Protective (HR < 1)" = "#457B9D")) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "log₂(Hazard Ratio)",
         y = "-log₁₀(p-value)",
         colour = "Direction",
         caption = sprintf("n = %d significant genes | dashed line: p = 0.05",
                           nrow(df))) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}

vol_plot <- plot_volcano(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L)
if (!is.null(vol_plot)) {
  ggsave("GBM_Volcano_Plot.png", vol_plot,
         width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 150)
  cat("📈 Volcano plot saved: GBM_Volcano_Plot.png\n")
}

# ── Plot B: Top-20 HR bar plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────
if ("HR" %in% colnames(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L) &&
    "Gene_Symbol" %in% colnames(Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L)) {

  df_top <- Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L %>%
    filter(!is.na(Gene_Symbol)) %>%
    mutate(Gene = Gene_Symbol,
           log2HR = log2(HR),
           Direction = ifelse(HR > 1, "High-risk", "Protective")) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(log2HR))) %>%
    head(20)

  hr_bar <- ggplot(df_top, aes(x = reorder(Gene, log2HR),
                                y = log2HR, fill = Direction)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("High-risk"  = "#E63946",
                                  "Protective" = "#457B9D")) +
    labs(title = "TCGA-GBM: Top 20 Survival Genes by Hazard Ratio",
         x = NULL, y = "log₂(Hazard Ratio)",
         fill = "Effect") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

  ggsave("GBM_Top20_HR_Barplot.png", hr_bar,
         width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 150)
  cat("📈 HR bar plot saved: GBM_Top20_HR_Barplot.png\n")
}

# ── Plot C: C-index comparison across models ─────────────────────────────────
if (nrow(model_perf_df) > 0) {
  perf_long <- model_perf_df %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(Train_C_index, Test_C_index),
                        names_to = "Set", values_to = "C_index") %>%
    mutate(Set = gsub("_C_index", "", Set))

  perf_plot <- ggplot(perf_long,
                      aes(x = Model, y = C_index, fill = Set)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Train" = "#2A9D8F", "Test" = "#E76F51")) +
    ylim(0, 1) +
    labs(title = "TCGA-GBM: MTLR Model Performance (C-index)",
         x = "Model", y = "C-index", fill = "Dataset") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

  ggsave("GBM_Model_CIndex_Comparison.png", perf_plot,
         width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 150)
  cat("📈 C-index comparison plot saved: GBM_Model_CIndex_Comparison.png\n")
}

# ── Plot D: Kaplan-Meier on full dataset (reference) ─────────────────────────
km_ref <- tryCatch({
  km_fit <- survfit(Surv(OS_month, OS) ~ 1, data = New_data)
  km_plot_ref <- ggsurvplot(km_fit,
    data        = New_data,
    risk.table  = TRUE,
    conf.int    = TRUE,
    palette     = "#2A9D8F",
    title       = "TCGA-GBM: Overall Survival (Full Cohort, KM Estimate)",
    xlab        = "Time (months)",
    ylab        = "Survival Probability",
    ggtheme     = theme_bw(base_size = 12),
    risk.table.height = 0.25
  )
  png("GBM_KM_Full_Cohort.png", width = 900, height = 650, res = 120)
  print(km_plot_ref)
  dev.off()
  cat("📈 Full-cohort KM plot saved: GBM_KM_Full_Cohort.png\n")
}, error = function(e) cat("⚠️  KM reference plot failed:", e$message, "\n"))

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# FINAL EXPORT: COMPREHENSIVE EXCEL WORKBOOK
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n💾 Writing comprehensive Excel workbook...\n")

wb_final <- createWorkbook()

# Sheet 1: Survival genes
addWorksheet(wb_final, "Survival_Genes")
writeData(wb_final, "Survival_Genes", Univariate_Suv_Sig_G_L, rowNames = TRUE)

# Sheet 2: LASSO genes (if available)
if (!is.null(Train_Lasso_key_variables)) {
  addWorksheet(wb_final, "LASSO_Genes")
  writeData(wb_final, "LASSO_Genes", Train_Lasso_key_variables, rowNames = FALSE)
}

# Sheet 3: Model performance
addWorksheet(wb_final, "Model_Performance")
writeData(wb_final, "Model_Performance", model_perf_df, rowNames = FALSE)

# Sheet 4: Significant clinical features
if (!is.null(Uni_Sur_Sig_clin_List) && nrow(Uni_Sur_Sig_clin_List) > 0) {
  addWorksheet(wb_final, "Significant_Clinical")
  writeData(wb_final, "Significant_Clinical", Uni_Sur_Sig_clin_List, rowNames = TRUE)
}

# Sheet 5: PH test summary
if (!is.null(PH_Summary) && nrow(PH_Summary) > 0) {
  addWorksheet(wb_final, "PH_Test_Summary")
  writeData(wb_final, "PH_Test_Summary", PH_Summary, rowNames = FALSE)
}

saveWorkbook(wb_final, "GBM_CPSM_Complete_Results.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
cat("💾 Complete results workbook: GBM_CPSM_Complete_Results.xlsx\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SESSION INFO
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n📋 Session Info\n")
cat("================\n")
sessionInfo()

cat("\n\n🎉 CPSM TCGA-GBM PIPELINE COMPLETE!\n")
cat("=======================================\n")
cat("Outputs generated:\n")
cat("  CSV:   GBM_Univariate_Survival_Genes_Full.csv\n")
cat("  CSV:   GBM_LASSO_selected_genes.csv\n")
cat("  CSV:   GBM_Model_Performance.csv\n")
cat("  XLSX:  GBM_Univariate_Survival_Genes_Full.xlsx\n")
cat("  XLSX:  GBM_CPSM_Complete_Results.xlsx\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_Volcano_Plot.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_Top20_HR_Barplot.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_Model_CIndex_Comparison.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_KM_Full_Cohort.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_KM_Overlay_Plot.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_Survival_Curves_All.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_Survival_Curves_Highlighted.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_MeanMedian_Survival_All.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_MeanMedian_Survival_Highlighted.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_LASSO_lambda_plot.png\n")
cat("  PNG:   GBM_Nomogram.png\n")
```


not sure what this is 
```{r}
# ========================================================================
# DATA PREPROCESSING & CPSM FORMAT CONVERSION
# ========================================================================

prepare_cpsm_format <- function(processed_data) {
  cat("Converting to CPSM format...\n")
  
  clinical_df <- processed_data$clinical
  expression_mat <- processed_data$expression
  
  # Create CPSM clinical structure
  cpsm_clinical <- data.frame(
    Age = as.numeric(clinical_df$age_at_index),
    Gender = tolower(clinical_df$gender),
    Race = ifelse(is.na(clinical_df$race), "not_reported", tolower(clinical_df$race)),
    
    # GBM-specific features
    Subtype = determine_gbm_subtype(clinical_df),
    WHO_Grade = ifelse("who_cns_grade" %in% colnames(clinical_df), 
                       clinical_df$who_cns_grade, "Grade IV"),
    
    # Sample info
    Sample_Type = ifelse("sample_type" %in% colnames(clinical_df),
                        clinical_df$sample_type, "Primary Tumor"),
    Cancer_Type = "GBM"
  )
  
  # Process survival data
  survival_data <- process_survival_endpoints(clinical_df)
  cpsm_clinical <- cbind(cpsm_clinical, survival_data)
  
  # Set consistent row names
  rownames(cpsm_clinical) <- rownames(clinical_df)
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ CPSM format created: %d samples, %d clinical variables\n", 
              nrow(cpsm_clinical), ncol(cpsm_clinical)))
  
  return(list(
    clinical = cpsm_clinical,
    expression = expression_mat
  ))
}

determine_gbm_subtype <- function(clinical_df) {
  if ("paper_Subtype" %in% colnames(clinical_df)) {
    return(ifelse(is.na(clinical_df$paper_Subtype), "Classical", clinical_df$paper_Subtype))
  }
  return(rep("Classical", nrow(clinical_df)))
}

process_survival_endpoints <- function(clinical_df) {
  # Calculate survival time
  survival_days <- ifelse(
    !is.na(clinical_df$days_to_death) & clinical_df$days_to_death > 0,
    clinical_df$days_to_death,
    ifelse(!is.na(clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up) & clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up > 0,
           clinical_df$days_to_last_follow_up, NA)
  )
  
  # Create survival status
  survival_status <- ifelse(clinical_df$vital_status == "Dead", 1, 0)
  
  # GBM survival endpoints
  data.frame(
    OS_Status = survival_status,
    OS_Time = survival_days,
    DSS_Status = survival_status, 
    DSS_Time = survival_days
  )
}

# ========================================================================
# DATA QUALITY CONTROL
# ========================================================================

perform_quality_control <- function(cpsm_data) {
  cat("Performing quality control...\n")
  
  clinical_df <- cpsm_data$clinical
  expression_mat <- cpsm_data$expression
  
  # Filter complete survival cases
  complete_survival <- !is.na(clinical_df$OS_Status) & 
                      !is.na(clinical_df$OS_Time) & 
                      clinical_df$OS_Time > 0 &
                      !is.na(clinical_df$Age) &
                      clinical_df$Age > 0
  
  cat(sprintf("Complete survival data: %d/%d samples (%.1f%%)\n",
              sum(complete_survival), length(complete_survival),
              100 * mean(complete_survival)))
  
  clinical_clean <- clinical_df[complete_survival, ]
  
  # Match expression data  
  common_samples <- intersect(rownames(clinical_clean), colnames(expression_mat))
  clinical_final <- clinical_clean[common_samples, ]
  expression_final <- expression_mat[, common_samples]
  
  # Gene filtering
  gene_na_prop <- rowMeans(is.na(expression_final) | expression_final == 0)
  good_genes <- gene_na_prop < 0.8
  expression_filtered <- expression_final[good_genes, ]
  
  # Select top variable genes
  if (nrow(expression_filtered) > 8000) {
    gene_vars <- apply(expression_filtered, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_genes <- order(gene_vars, decreasing = TRUE)[1:8000]
    expression_filtered <- expression_filtered[top_genes, ]
  }
  
  # Transform expression data
  expression_filtered[expression_filtered <= 0] <- 1e-6
  expression_log <- log2(expression_filtered + 1)
  expression_transposed <- t(expression_log)
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Final dataset: %d samples, %d genes\n", 
              nrow(expression_transposed), ncol(expression_transposed)))
  
  # Summary statistics
  summary_stats <- list(
    n_samples = nrow(clinical_final),
    n_genes = ncol(expression_transposed),
    n_events = sum(clinical_final$OS_Status),
    median_age = median(clinical_final$Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_survival = median(clinical_final$OS_Time, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
  
  return(list(
    clinical = clinical_final,
    expression = expression_transposed,
    summary = summary_stats
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# GENE DISCOVERY & FEATURE SELECTION
# ========================================================================

discover_survival_genes <- function(qc_data, n_genes = 100) {
  cat("Discovering survival-associated genes...\n")
  
  clinical_df <- qc_data$clinical
  expression_df <- as.data.frame(qc_data$expression)
  
  # Prepare combined dataset
  combined_data <- cbind(clinical_df, expression_df)
  gene_names <- colnames(expression_df)
  
  # Univariate Cox regression
  cox_results <- data.frame(
    Gene = character(0),
    HR = numeric(0), 
    CI_Lower = numeric(0),
    CI_Upper = numeric(0),
    P_Value = numeric(0)
  )
  
  cat("Running Cox regression analysis...\n")
  pb <- txtProgressBar(0, length(gene_names), style = 3)
  
  for (i in seq_along(gene_names)) {
    gene <- gene_names[i]
    
    tryCatch({
      surv_obj <- Surv(combined_data$OS_Time, combined_data$OS_Status)
      cox_fit <- coxph(surv_obj ~ combined_data[[gene]])
      
      coef_summary <- summary(cox_fit)$coefficients
      conf_int <- summary(cox_fit)$conf.int
      
      cox_results <- rbind(cox_results, data.frame(
        Gene = gene,
        HR = exp(coef_summary[1]),
        CI_Lower = conf_int[3],
        CI_Upper = conf_int[4], 
        P_Value = coef_summary[5]
      ))
    }, error = function(e) {
      # Skip problematic genes
    })
    
    setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)
  }
  close(pb)
  
  # Multiple testing correction
  cox_results$P_Adjusted <- p.adjust(cox_results$P_Value, method = "BH")
  cox_results <- cox_results[order(cox_results$P_Value), ]
  
  # Select top genes
  significant_genes <- cox_results[cox_results$P_Adjusted < 0.05, ]
  
  if (nrow(significant_genes) == 0) {
    cat("No significant genes found. Using top genes by p-value.\n")
    selected_genes <- head(cox_results, n_genes)
  } else {
    selected_genes <- head(significant_genes, n_genes)
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Gene discovery complete:\n"))
  cat(sprintf("  Total genes tested: %d\n", nrow(cox_results)))
  cat(sprintf("  Significant genes (FDR < 0.05): %d\n", nrow(significant_genes)))
  cat(sprintf("  Selected for modeling: %d\n", nrow(selected_genes)))
  
  return(list(
    all_results = cox_results,
    significant = significant_genes,
    selected = selected_genes
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# SURVIVAL MODELING
# ========================================================================

train_survival_models <- function(qc_data, gene_results, test_fraction = 0.3) {
  cat("Training survival prediction models...\n")
  
  clinical_df <- qc_data$clinical
  expression_df <- as.data.frame(qc_data$expression)
  selected_genes <- gene_results$selected$Gene
  
  # Prepare modeling dataset
  model_data <- cbind(
    clinical_df[, c("Age", "Gender", "OS_Status", "OS_Time")],
    expression_df[, selected_genes, drop = FALSE]
  )
  
  # Train-test split
  set.seed(42)
  n_test <- floor(nrow(model_data) * test_fraction)
  test_idx <- sample(nrow(model_data), n_test)
  
  train_data <- model_data[-test_idx, ]
  test_data <- model_data[test_idx, ]
  
  cat(sprintf("Data split: %d train, %d test\n", nrow(train_data), nrow(test_data)))
  
  # Train models
  models <- list()
  
  cat("Training Cox model...\n")
  models$cox <- train_cox_model(train_data, test_data)
  
  cat("Training Random Forest...\n")
  models$rsf <- train_rsf_model(train_data, test_data)
  
  cat("Training Elastic Net...\n") 
  models$enet <- train_enet_model(train_data, test_data)
  
  # Compare performance
  performance <- data.frame(
    Model = c("Cox_PH", "Random_Forest", "Elastic_Net"),
    Train_C_Index = c(models$cox$train_c, models$rsf$train_c, models$enet$train_c),
    Test_C_Index = c(models$cox$test_c, models$rsf$test_c, models$enet$test_c)
  )
  
  best_model <- performance$Model[which.max(performance$Test_C_Index)]
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Model training complete:\n"))
  print(performance)
  cat(sprintf("Best model: %s\n", best_model))
  
  return(list(
    performance = performance,
    models = models,
    best_model = best_model
  ))
}

train_cox_model <- function(train_data, test_data) {
  train_surv <- Surv(train_data$OS_Time, train_data$OS_Status)
  test_surv <- Surv(test_data$OS_Time, test_data$OS_Status)
  
  train_features <- train_data[, !names(train_data) %in% c("OS_Status", "OS_Time")]
  test_features <- test_data[, !names(test_data) %in% c("OS_Status", "OS_Time")]
  
  cox_fit <- coxph(train_surv ~ ., data = train_features)
  
  train_c <- summary(cox_fit)$concordance["C"]
  test_pred <- predict(cox_fit, newdata = test_features, type = "risk")
  test_c <- concordance(test_surv ~ test_pred)$concordance
  
  return(list(model = cox_fit, train_c = train_c, test_c = test_c))
}

train_rsf_model <- function(train_data, test_data) {
  train_features <- train_data[, !names(train_data) %in% c("OS_Status", "OS_Time")]
  test_features <- test_data[, !names(test_data) %in% c("OS_Status", "OS_Time")]
  
  rsf_train <- cbind(train_features, time = train_data$OS_Time, status = train_data$OS_Status)
  
  rsf_fit <- rfsrc(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = rsf_train, ntree = 500)
  rsf_pred <- predict(rsf_fit, newdata = test_features)
  
  train_c <- 1 - rsf_fit$err.rate[rsf_fit$ntree]
  test_surv <- Surv(test_data$OS_Time, test_data$OS_Status)
  test_c <- concordance(test_surv ~ rsf_pred$predicted)$concordance
  
  return(list(model = rsf_fit, train_c = train_c, test_c = test_c))
}

train_enet_model <- function(train_data, test_data) {
  train_features <- train_data[, !names(train_data) %in% c("OS_Status", "OS_Time")]
  test_features <- test_data[, !names(test_data) %in% c("OS_Status", "OS_Time")]
  
  x_train <- as.matrix(train_features)
  x_test <- as.matrix(test_features)
  y_train <- Surv(train_data$OS_Time, train_data$OS_Status)
  y_test <- Surv(test_data$OS_Time, test_data$OS_Status)
  
  cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, family = "cox", alpha = 0.5)
  
  train_pred <- predict(cv_fit, newx = x_train, s = "lambda.min", type = "link")
  test_pred <- predict(cv_fit, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.min", type = "link")
  
  train_c <- concordance(y_train ~ train_pred[, 1])$concordance
  test_c <- concordance(y_test ~ test_pred[, 1])$concordance
  
  return(list(model = cv_fit, train_c = train_c, test_c = test_c))
}

# ========================================================================
# REPORT GENERATION
# ========================================================================

generate_comprehensive_report <- function(all_results, output_dir) {
  cat("Generating comprehensive report...\n")
  
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  # Save results
  write.csv(all_results$model_results$performance, 
            file.path(output_dir, "model_performance.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  write.csv(all_results$gene_results$selected,
            file.path(output_dir, "top_genes.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  write.csv(all_results$duplicate_handling$handling_log,
            file.path(output_dir, "duplicate_handling_log.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Markdown report
  report <- sprintf('
# GBM CPSM Analysis Report
Generated: %s

## Duplicate Analysis Summary
- Original samples: %d
- After duplicate handling: %d
- Strategy used:
  - Biological replicates: %s
  - Technical replicates: %s

## Dataset Summary  
- Samples: %d
- Genes: %d
- Events: %d
- Median survival: %.1f days

## Model Performance
%s

## Top 10 Genes
%s

Generated by GBM CPSM Pipeline with Advanced Duplicate Analysis
',
    Sys.Date(),
    nrow(all_results$duplicate_results$clinical_data),
    nrow(all_results$qc_data$clinical),
    all_results$duplicate_handling$strategy_used$biological,
    all_results$duplicate_handling$strategy_used$technical,
    all_results$qc_data$summary$n_samples,
    all_results$qc_data$summary$n_genes,
    all_results$qc_data$summary$n_events,
    all_results$qc_data$summary$median_survival,
    paste(capture.output(print(all_results$model_results$performance)), collapse = "\n"),
    paste(head(all_results$gene_results$selected$Gene, 10), collapse = ", ")
  )
  
  writeLines(report, file.path(output_dir, "Analysis_Report.md"))
  
  cat(sprintf("✓ Reports saved to: %s\n", output_dir))
}

# ========================================================================
# MAIN PIPELINE WITH DUPLICATE ANALYSIS
# ========================================================================

run_complete_analysis_with_duplicate_understanding <- function(
  data_source = "tcga",
  output_dir = "GBM_Results",
  biological_strategy = NULL,
  technical_strategy = NULL,
  interactive = TRUE
) {
  
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  GBM CPSM ANALYSIS WITH INTELLIGENT DUPLICATE HANDLING      \n")
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
  
  # Step 1: Load data
  cat("STEP 1: Loading TCGA-GBM data...\n")
  if (data_source == "tcga") {
    # Look for cached data first
    cache_files <- list.files(".", pattern = "TCGA.*GBM.*\\.rda", full.names = TRUE)
    if (length(cache_files) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("Found cached file: %s\n", cache_files[1]))
      load(cache_files[1])
      se_data <- data
    } else {
      cat("No cached data found. Please download TCGA data first.\n")
      return(NULL)
    }
  } else {
    cat("Generating simulated data...\n")
    se_data <- generate_simulated_data()
  }
  
  # Step 2: COMPREHENSIVE duplicate analysis
  cat("\nSTEP 2: Comprehensive duplicate analysis...\n")
  duplicate_results <- perform_comprehensive_duplicate_analysis(se_data)
  
  # Step 3: Interactive strategy selection or use provided strategies
  if (interactive && (is.null(biological_strategy) || is.null(technical_strategy))) {
    cat("\n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
    cat("║  CHOOSE YOUR DUPLICATE HANDLING STRATEGY                    ║\n")
    cat("╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")
    
    if (is.null(biological_strategy)) {
      cat("Biological replicates strategy:\n")
      cat("  1. priority_selection (recommended for most cases)\n")
      cat("  2. average (if high correlation)\n")
      cat("  3. keep_separate (for longitudinal studies)\n")
      biological_strategy <- readline("Enter choice (1-3): ")
      biological_strategy <- switch(biological_strategy,
        "1" = "priority_selection",
        "2" = "average",
        "3" = "keep_separate",
        "priority_selection"  # default
      )
    }
    
    if (is.null(technical_strategy)) {
      cat("\nTechnical replicates strategy:\n")
      cat("  1. average (recommended)\n")
      cat("  2. select_best_quality\n")
      technical_strategy <- readline("Enter choice (1-2): ")
      technical_strategy <- switch(technical_strategy,
        "1" = "average",
        "2" = "select_best_quality",
        "average"  # default
      )
    }
  } else {
    # Use defaults if not provided
    if (is.null(biological_strategy)) biological_strategy <- "priority_selection"
    if (is.null(technical_strategy)) technical_strategy <- "average"
  }
  
  # Step 4: Apply chosen strategy
  cat("\nSTEP 3: Applying duplicate handling strategy...\n")
  processed_data <- apply_duplicate_handling(
    duplicate_results,
    biological_strategy = biological_strategy,
    technical_strategy = technical_strategy
  )
  
  # Step 5: Continue with standard pipeline
  cat("\nSTEP 4: Data preprocessing...\n")
  cpsm_data <- prepare_cpsm_format(processed_data)
  
  cat("\nSTEP 5: Quality control...\n")
  qc_data <- perform_quality_control(cpsm_data)
  
  cat("\nSTEP 6: Gene discovery...\n")
  gene_results <- discover_survival_genes(qc_data, n_genes = 100)
  
  cat("\nSTEP 7: Model training...\n")
  model_results <- train_survival_models(qc_data, gene_results)
  
  # Compile results
  all_results <- list(
    duplicate_results = duplicate_results,
    duplicate_handling = processed_data,
    cpsm_data = cpsm_data,
    qc_data = qc_data,
    gene_results = gene_results,
    model_results = model_results
  )
  
  # Generate report
  cat("\nSTEP 8: Generating reports...\n")
  generate_comprehensive_report(all_results, output_dir)
  
  cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  ANALYSIS COMPLETE!                                          \n")
  cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat(sprintf("Results saved to: %s\n", output_dir))
  
  return(all_results)
}

# Simulated data generator
generate_simulated_data <- function(n_samples = 200, n_genes = 5000) {
  set.seed(123)
  
  clinical_data <- data.frame(
    submitter_id = paste0("TCGA-GBM-", sprintf("%04d", 1:n_samples), "-01A"),
    age_at_index = pmax(18, rnorm(n_samples, 55, 12)),
    gender = sample(c("male", "female"), n_samples, replace = TRUE),
    vital_status = sample(c("Dead", "Alive"), n_samples, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.75, 0.25)),
    sample_type = "Primary Tumor"
  )
  
  clinical_data$days_to_death <- ifelse(
    clinical_data$vital_status == "Dead",
    pmax(30, rexp(n_samples, 1/450)),
    NA
  )
  
  clinical_data$days_to_last_follow_up <- ifelse(
    clinical_data$vital_status == "Alive",
    pmax(30, rexp(n_samples, 1/800)),
    clinical_data$days_to_death
  )
  
  gene_names <- paste0("ENSG", sprintf("%011d", 1:n_genes))
  expression_data <- matrix(
    rlnorm(n_samples * n_genes, meanlog = 6, sdlog = 1.5),
    nrow = n_genes, ncol = n_samples,
    dimnames = list(gene_names, clinical_data$submitter_id)
  )
  
  SummarizedExperiment(
    assays = list(fpkm_unstrand = expression_data),
    colData = clinical_data
  )
}

# ========================================================================
# USAGE INSTRUCTIONS
# ========================================================================

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  GBM CPSM PIPELINE WITH INTELLIGENT DUPLICATE ANALYSIS       \n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

cat("🔬 KEY FEATURES:\n")
cat("• Comprehensive duplicate analysis BEFORE removal\n")
cat("• Distinguishes biological vs technical replicates\n")
cat("• Correlation-based quality assessment\n")
cat("• Temporal/longitudinal pattern detection\n")
cat("• Flexible handling strategies\n")
cat("• Complete audit trail\n\n")

cat("📊 USAGE:\n\n")

cat("1. ANALYZE DUPLICATES FIRST (Recommended):\n")
cat("   results <- run_complete_analysis_with_duplicate_understanding()\n\n")

cat("2. NON-INTERACTIVE WITH CHOSEN STRATEGY:\n")
cat("   results <- run_complete_analysis_with_duplicate_understanding(\n")
cat("     biological_strategy = 'priority_selection',\n")
cat("     technical_strategy = 'average',\n")
cat("     interactive = FALSE\n")
cat("   )\n\n")

cat("3. JUST ANALYZE DUPLICATES (No modeling):\n")
cat("   # Load your data first\n")
cat("   load('TCGA_GBM_data.rda')\n")
cat("   dup_analysis <- perform_comprehensive_duplicate_analysis(data)\n\n")

cat("Ready to analyze your GBM data with intelligent duplicate handling!\n\n")#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
# ========================================================================
# COMPLETE GBM (GLIOBLASTOMA) CPSM ANALYSIS PIPELINE
# ========================================================================
# Advanced pipeline with comprehensive duplicate analysis BEFORE removal
# Strategy: Analyze → Understand → Then Decide on handling approach
# ========================================================================

# Load required libraries
cat("Loading required libraries...\n")
required_packages <- c(
  "TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt",
  "survival", "survminer", "randomForestSRC", 
  "glmnet", "caret", "dplyr", "ggplot2"
)

# Install missing packages
for (pkg in required_packages) {
  if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE)) {
    cat(sprintf("Installing %s...\n", pkg))
    if (pkg %in% c("TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt")) {
      if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
      BiocManager::install(pkg, ask = FALSE)
    } else {
      install.packages(pkg)
    }
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }
}

cat("✓ All packages loaded successfully\n\n")

# ========================================================================
# COMPREHENSIVE DUPLICATE ANALYSIS - ANALYZE BEFORE REMOVING
# ========================================================================

perform_comprehensive_duplicate_analysis <- function(se_data) {
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  COMPREHENSIVE DUPLICATE ANALYSIS - UNDERSTANDING YOUR DATA   \n")
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
  
  clinical_data <- as.data.frame(colData(se_data))
  expression_data <- assay(se_data, "fpkm_unstrand")
  
  # Extract TCGA barcode components
  cat("STEP 1: Parsing TCGA Barcodes\n")
  cat("─────────────────────────────────\n")
  
  if ("submitter_id" %in% colnames(clinical_data)) {
    clinical_data$patient_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 12)
    clinical_data$sample_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 16)
    clinical_data$vial_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 19)
    clinical_data$portion_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 20)
    clinical_data$analyte_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 21)
    clinical_data$plate_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 25)
  } else {
    stop("Cannot extract TCGA barcode structure from data")
  }
  
  cat("TCGA Barcode Structure:\n")
  cat("  Example: TCGA-02-0001-01C-01D-0182-01\n")
  cat("           └─Patient─┘ └Sample└Vial└Portion└Analyte└Plate\n\n")
  
  # Analyze duplicate levels
  cat("STEP 2: Identifying Duplicate Levels\n")
  cat("─────────────────────────────────────\n")
  
  patient_counts <- table(clinical_data$patient_id)
  sample_counts <- table(clinical_data$sample_id)
  vial_counts <- table(clinical_data$vial_id)
  portion_counts <- table(clinical_data$portion_id)
  analyte_counts <- table(clinical_data$analyte_id)
  
  cat(sprintf("Total aliquots: %d\n", nrow(clinical_data)))
  cat(sprintf("Unique patients: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$patient_id))))
  cat(sprintf("Unique samples: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$sample_id))))
  cat(sprintf("Unique vials: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$vial_id))))
  cat(sprintf("Unique portions: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$portion_id))))
  cat(sprintf("Unique analytes: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$analyte_id))))
  
  # Identify different types of duplicates
  cat("\nSTEP 3: Categorizing Duplicate Types\n")
  cat("─────────────────────────────────────\n")
  
  duplicate_analysis <- list()
  
  # Type 1: Multiple samples per patient (biological replicates)
  patients_with_multiple_samples <- names(patient_counts[patient_counts > 1])
  if (length(patients_with_multiple_samples) > 0) {
    bio_reps <- clinical_data[clinical_data$patient_id %in% patients_with_multiple_samples, ]
    
    cat(sprintf("\n✓ TYPE 1: BIOLOGICAL REPLICATES (Multiple samples per patient)\n"))
    cat(sprintf("  Patients with multiple samples: %d\n", length(patients_with_multiple_samples)))
    cat(sprintf("  Total biological replicates: %d\n", nrow(bio_reps)))
    
    if ("sample_type" %in% colnames(clinical_data)) {
      cat("\n  Sample type breakdown:\n")
      type_summary <- bio_reps %>%
        group_by(patient_id) %>%
        summarise(
          n_samples = n(),
          sample_types = paste(unique(sample_type), collapse = ", "),
          .groups = 'drop'
        )
      print(as.data.frame(head(type_summary, 10)))
      
      cat("\n  Overall sample type distribution:\n")
      print(table(bio_reps$sample_type))
    }
    
    # Check if these are time series (different collection dates)
    if ("days_to_collection" %in% colnames(clinical_data) || 
        "days_to_sample_procurement" %in% colnames(clinical_data)) {
      cat("\n  ⚠️  LONGITUDINAL SAMPLES DETECTED\n")
      cat("  These may be samples collected at different time points\n")
      cat("  Consider: Averaging, selecting by time point, or modeling temporal changes\n")
    }
    
    duplicate_analysis$biological_replicates <- bio_reps
  }
  
  # Type 2: Technical replicates (same sample, multiple aliquots)
  samples_with_multiple_aliquots <- names(sample_counts[sample_counts > 1])
  if (length(samples_with_multiple_aliquots) > 0) {
    tech_reps <- clinical_data[clinical_data$sample_id %in% samples_with_multiple_aliquots, ]
    
    cat(sprintf("\n✓ TYPE 2: TECHNICAL REPLICATES (Multiple aliquots per sample)\n"))
    cat(sprintf("  Samples with multiple aliquots: %d\n", length(samples_with_multiple_aliquots)))
    cat(sprintf("  Total technical replicates: %d\n", nrow(tech_reps)))
    
    # Show example
    example_sample <- tech_reps$sample_id[1]
    example_aliquots <- tech_reps[tech_reps$sample_id == example_sample, "submitter_id"]
    cat(sprintf("\n  Example - Sample: %s\n", example_sample))
    cat("  Aliquots:\n")
    for (aliquot in example_aliquots) {
      cat(sprintf("    - %s\n", aliquot))
    }
    
    duplicate_analysis$technical_replicates <- tech_reps
  }
  
  # Analyze expression correlation for technical replicates
  cat("\nSTEP 4: Correlation Analysis (Technical Replicate Quality Check)\n")
  cat("────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  
  correlation_results <- analyze_duplicate_correlations(
    clinical_data, expression_data, duplicate_analysis
  )
  
  duplicate_analysis$correlations <- correlation_results
  
  # Analyze temporal patterns
  cat("\nSTEP 5: Temporal and Clinical Context Analysis\n")
  cat("───────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  
  temporal_analysis <- analyze_temporal_patterns(clinical_data, duplicate_analysis)
  duplicate_analysis$temporal <- temporal_analysis
  
  # Generate recommendations
  cat("\nSTEP 6: Generating Duplicate Handling Recommendations\n")
  cat("──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
  
  recommendations <- generate_handling_recommendations(duplicate_analysis, clinical_data)
  
  cat("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  DUPLICATE ANALYSIS COMPLETE                                  \n")
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
  
  return(list(
    analysis = duplicate_analysis,
    recommendations = recommendations,
    clinical_data = clinical_data,
    expression_data = expression_data
  ))
}

analyze_duplicate_correlations <- function(clinical_data, expression_data, duplicate_analysis) {
  cat("Calculating expression correlations between duplicates...\n")
  
  correlations <- list()
  
  # Function to calculate pairwise correlations
  calc_pairwise_correlations <- function(sample_ids) {
    if (length(sample_ids) < 2) return(NULL)
    
    # Match sample IDs to expression data columns
    common_samples <- intersect(sample_ids, colnames(expression_data))
    if (length(common_samples) < 2) return(NULL)
    
    expr_subset <- expression_data[, common_samples]
    
    # Remove zero-variance genes
    gene_vars <- apply(expr_subset, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
    expr_subset <- expr_subset[!is.na(gene_vars) & gene_vars > 0, ]
    
    if (nrow(expr_subset) < 100) return(NULL)  # Need sufficient genes
    
    # Calculate correlation matrix
    cor_matrix <- cor(expr_subset, use = "complete.obs", method = "spearman")
    
    # Extract unique pairwise correlations
    n <- ncol(cor_matrix)
    pairs_idx <- which(upper.tri(cor_matrix), arr.ind = TRUE)
    
    if (nrow(pairs_idx) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    data.frame(
      sample1 = colnames(cor_matrix)[pairs_idx[, 1]],
      sample2 = colnames(cor_matrix)[pairs_idx[, 2]],
      correlation = cor_matrix[pairs_idx],
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
  
  # Analyze biological replicates
  if ("biological_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis) && 
      nrow(duplicate_analysis$biological_replicates) > 0) {
    
    cat("\nBiological replicate correlations:\n")
    bio_groups <- split(
      rownames(duplicate_analysis$biological_replicates),
      duplicate_analysis$biological_replicates$patient_id
    )
    
    bio_cors <- lapply(bio_groups, calc_pairwise_correlations)
    bio_cors <- do.call(rbind, bio_cors[!sapply(bio_cors, is.null)])
    
    if (!is.null(bio_cors) && nrow(bio_cors) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  Mean correlation: %.3f\n", mean(bio_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)))
      cat(sprintf("  Median correlation: %.3f\n", median(bio_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)))
      cat(sprintf("  Range: %.3f - %.3f\n", 
                  min(bio_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE),
                  max(bio_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)))
      
      # Flag suspicious cases
      high_cor_bio <- bio_cors[bio_cors$correlation > 0.98, ]
      if (nrow(high_cor_bio) > 0) {
        cat(sprintf("\n  ⚠️  WARNING: %d biological replicate pairs have correlation > 0.98\n", 
                    nrow(high_cor_bio)))
        cat("  These may actually be technical replicates mislabeled as biological\n")
      }
      
      correlations$biological <- bio_cors
    }
  }
  
  # Analyze technical replicates
  if ("technical_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis) &&
      nrow(duplicate_analysis$technical_replicates) > 0) {
    
    cat("\nTechnical replicate correlations:\n")
    tech_groups <- split(
      rownames(duplicate_analysis$technical_replicates),
      duplicate_analysis$technical_replicates$sample_id
    )
    
    tech_cors <- lapply(tech_groups, calc_pairwise_correlations)
    tech_cors <- do.call(rbind, tech_cors[!sapply(tech_cors, is.null)])
    
    if (!is.null(tech_cors) && nrow(tech_cors) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("  Mean correlation: %.3f\n", mean(tech_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)))
      cat(sprintf("  Median correlation: %.3f\n", median(tech_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)))
      cat(sprintf("  Range: %.3f - %.3f\n",
                  min(tech_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE),
                  max(tech_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)))
      
      # Flag quality issues
      low_cor_tech <- tech_cors[tech_cors$correlation < 0.90, ]
      if (nrow(low_cor_tech) > 0) {
        cat(sprintf("\n  ⚠️  WARNING: %d technical replicate pairs have correlation < 0.90\n",
                    nrow(low_cor_tech)))
        cat("  This suggests quality issues or sample mix-ups\n")
      }
      
      correlations$technical <- tech_cors
    }
  }
  
  return(correlations)
}

analyze_temporal_patterns <- function(clinical_data, duplicate_analysis) {
  cat("Analyzing temporal patterns in duplicates...\n")
  
  temporal_info <- list()
  
  # Check for time-related columns
  time_cols <- c("days_to_collection", "days_to_sample_procurement", 
                 "year_of_diagnosis", "days_to_birth")
  available_time_cols <- intersect(time_cols, colnames(clinical_data))
  
  if (length(available_time_cols) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  Available temporal information: %s\n", 
                paste(available_time_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    temporal_info$has_temporal_data <- TRUE
    temporal_info$time_columns <- available_time_cols
  } else {
    cat("  No temporal information available in dataset\n")
    temporal_info$has_temporal_data <- FALSE
  }
  
  # Analyze sample collection patterns
  if ("biological_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis) &&
      "sample_type" %in% colnames(clinical_data)) {
    
    bio_reps <- duplicate_analysis$biological_replicates
    
    # Check if different sample types represent progression/recurrence
    sample_type_patterns <- bio_reps %>%
      group_by(patient_id) %>%
      summarise(
        n_samples = n(),
        has_primary = any(grepl("Primary", sample_type, ignore.case = TRUE)),
        has_recurrent = any(grepl("Recurrent", sample_type, ignore.case = TRUE)),
        has_metastatic = any(grepl("Metastatic", sample_type, ignore.case = TRUE)),
        .groups = 'drop'
      )
    
    primary_recurrent <- sum(sample_type_patterns$has_primary & sample_type_patterns$has_recurrent)
    
    if (primary_recurrent > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("\n  ✓ LONGITUDINAL STUDY DETECTED\n"))
      cat(sprintf("  %d patients have both primary and recurrent samples\n", primary_recurrent))
      cat("  These represent disease progression and should be handled carefully\n")
      temporal_info$is_longitudinal <- TRUE
      temporal_info$longitudinal_count <- primary_recurrent
    }
  }
  
  return(temporal_info)
}

generate_handling_recommendations <- function(duplicate_analysis, clinical_data) {
  cat("\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n")
  cat("│  DUPLICATE HANDLING RECOMMENDATIONS                         │\n")
  cat("└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n\n")
  
  recommendations <- list()
  
  # Biological replicates recommendations
  if ("biological_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis)) {
    cat("📊 BIOLOGICAL REPLICATES (Multiple samples per patient):\n")
    cat("─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
    
    bio_cors <- duplicate_analysis$correlations$biological
    
    if (!is.null(bio_cors)) {
      median_cor <- median(bio_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      if (median_cor > 0.95) {
        cat("  Strategy: AVERAGE or SELECT ONE\n")
        cat("  Reason: High correlation suggests these are essentially identical\n")
        cat("  Options:\n")
        cat("    1. Average expression values across replicates\n")
        cat("    2. Select replicate with highest RNA quality/read count\n")
        cat("    3. Select primary tumor over recurrent/metastatic\n")
        recommendations$biological <- "average_or_select"
        
      } else if (median_cor > 0.70 && median_cor <= 0.95) {
        cat("  Strategy: KEEP AS SEPARATE OBSERVATIONS\n")
        cat("  Reason: Moderate correlation suggests biological differences\n")
        cat("  Options:\n")
        cat("    1. Keep all samples as independent observations\n")
        cat("    2. Model with patient as random effect\n")
        cat("    3. Use mixed-effects survival models\n")
        recommendations$biological <- "keep_separate"
        
      } else {
        cat("  Strategy: INVESTIGATE CAREFULLY\n")
        cat("  Reason: Low correlation suggests:\n")
        cat("    - Tumor heterogeneity\n")
        cat("    - Disease progression/evolution\n")
        cat("    - Technical quality issues\n")
        cat("  Options:\n")
        cat("    1. Select highest quality sample\n")
        cat("    2. Analyze as longitudinal study if temporal data available\n")
        cat("    3. Investigate for sample mix-ups\n")
        recommendations$biological <- "investigate"
      }
    } else {
      cat("  Strategy: SELECT BY PRIORITY\n")
      cat("  Priority order:\n")
      cat("    1. Primary Tumor > Recurrent > Metastatic\n")
      cat("    2. Higher RNA quality/read count\n")
      cat("    3. Earlier time point if longitudinal\n")
      recommendations$biological <- "priority_selection"
    }
    
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  # Technical replicates recommendations
  if ("technical_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis)) {
    cat("🔬 TECHNICAL REPLICATES (Multiple aliquots per sample):\n")
    cat("────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
    
    tech_cors <- duplicate_analysis$correlations$technical
    
    if (!is.null(tech_cors)) {
      median_cor <- median(tech_cors$correlation, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      if (median_cor > 0.95) {
        cat("  Strategy: AVERAGE (Preferred) or SELECT ONE\n")
        cat("  Reason: High correlation indicates good technical reproducibility\n")
        cat("  Recommended: Average expression values to reduce technical noise\n")
        recommendations$technical <- "average"
        
      } else if (median_cor > 0.85 && median_cor <= 0.95) {
        cat("  Strategy: AVERAGE WITH CAUTION\n")
        cat("  Reason: Moderate correlation suggests some technical variability\n")
        cat("  Recommended: Average but flag for quality review\n")
        recommendations$technical <- "average_with_caution"
        
      } else {
        cat("  Strategy: SELECT HIGHEST QUALITY\n")
        cat("  Reason: Low correlation indicates technical problems\n")
        cat("  Recommended: Select replicate with:\n")
        cat("    - Highest total read count\n")
        cat("    - Best RNA quality metrics\n")
        cat("    - Most genes detected\n")
        cat("  Consider excluding low-quality replicates entirely\n")
        recommendations$technical <- "select_best_quality"
      }
    } else {
      cat("  Strategy: AVERAGE\n")
      cat("  Recommended: Average technical replicates by default\n")
      recommendations$technical <- "average"
    }
    
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  # Longitudinal study recommendations
  if (!is.null(duplicate_analysis$temporal) && 
      !is.null(duplicate_analysis$temporal$is_longitudinal) &&
      duplicate_analysis$temporal$is_longitudinal) {
    
    cat("⏱️  LONGITUDINAL SAMPLES DETECTED:\n")
    cat("───────────────────────────────────\n")
    cat("  Strategy: SPECIALIZED LONGITUDINAL ANALYSIS\n")
    cat("  Options:\n")
    cat("    1. Analyze primary vs recurrent separately\n")
    cat("    2. Use time-to-event from diagnosis\n")
    cat("    3. Model disease progression trajectories\n")
    cat("    4. Study molecular evolution over time\n")
    recommendations$longitudinal <- "specialized_analysis"
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  cat("┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n")
  cat("│  RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS                                     │\n")
  cat("└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n\n")
  cat("1. Review the analysis results above\n")
  cat("2. Choose handling strategy based on your research question\n")
  cat("3. Run: apply_duplicate_handling() with your chosen strategy\n")
  cat("4. Validate results with subset analysis\n\n")
  
  return(recommendations)
}

# ========================================================================
# APPLY DUPLICATE HANDLING BASED ON CHOSEN STRATEGY
# ========================================================================

apply_duplicate_handling <- function(duplicate_results, 
                                    biological_strategy = "priority_selection",
                                    technical_strategy = "average",
                                    keep_longitudinal_separate = FALSE) {
  
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  APPLYING DUPLICATE HANDLING STRATEGY                        \n")
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
  
  clinical_data <- duplicate_results$clinical_data
  expression_data <- duplicate_results$expression_data
  duplicate_analysis <- duplicate_results$analysis
  
  selected_samples <- rownames(clinical_data)
  removed_samples <- character(0)
  handling_log <- data.frame(
    sample_id = character(),
    action = character(),
    reason = character(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Handle technical replicates first
  if ("technical_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis)) {
    cat("Processing technical replicates...\n")
    
    tech_reps <- duplicate_analysis$technical_replicates
    tech_groups <- split(rownames(tech_reps), tech_reps$sample_id)
    
    for (sample_id in names(tech_groups)) {
      aliquots <- tech_groups[[sample_id]]
      
      if (length(aliquots) > 1) {
        if (technical_strategy == "average") {
          # Average expression values
          common_aliquots <- intersect(aliquots, colnames(expression_data))
          if (length(common_aliquots) > 1) {
            avg_expr <- rowMeans(expression_data[, common_aliquots, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
            expression_data[, common_aliquots[1]] <- avg_expr
            
            # Keep first, remove others
            to_remove <- common_aliquots[-1]
            selected_samples <- setdiff(selected_samples, to_remove)
            removed_samples <- c(removed_samples, to_remove)
            
            for (sample in to_remove) {
              handling_log <- rbind(handling_log, data.frame(
                sample_id = sample,
                action = "averaged",
                reason = sprintf("Technical replicate averaged into %s", common_aliquots[1]),
                stringsAsFactors = FALSE
              ))
            }
          }
        } else if (technical_strategy == "select_best_quality") {
          # Select aliquot with most genes detected
          common_aliquots <- intersect(aliquots, colnames(expression_data))
          if (length(common_aliquots) > 1) {
            genes_detected <- colSums(expression_data[, common_aliquots, drop = FALSE] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
            best_aliquot <- names(which.max(genes_detected))
            
            to_remove <- setdiff(common_aliquots, best_aliquot)
            selected_samples <- setdiff(selected_samples, to_remove)
            removed_samples <- c(removed_samples, to_remove)
            
            for (sample in to_remove) {
              handling_log <- rbind(handling_log, data.frame(
                sample_id = sample,
                action = "removed",
                reason = sprintf("Lower quality than %s", best_aliquot),
                stringsAsFactors = FALSE
              ))
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("  Processed %d technical replicate groups\n", length(tech_groups)))
  }
  
  # Handle biological replicates
  if ("biological_replicates" %in% names(duplicate_analysis)) {
    cat("\nProcessing biological replicates...\n")
    
    bio_reps <- duplicate_analysis$biological_replicates
    bio_reps <- bio_reps[rownames(bio_reps) %in% selected_samples, ]  # Only still-selected samples
    
    if (nrow(bio_reps) > 0) {
      patient_groups <- split(rownames(bio_reps), bio_reps$patient_id)
      
      for (patient_id in names(patient_groups)) {
        patient_samples <- bio_reps[bio_reps$patient_id == patient_id, ]
        sample_ids <- rownames(patient_samples)
        
        if (length(sample_ids) > 1) {
          
          if (biological_strategy == "average") {
            # Average expression across biological replicates
            common_samples <- intersect(sample_ids, colnames(expression_data))
            if (length(common_samples) > 1) {
              avg_expr <- rowMeans(expression_data[, common_samples, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
              expression_data[, common_samples[1]] <- avg_expr
              
              to_remove <- common_samples[-1]
              selected_samples <- setdiff(selected_samples, to_remove)
              removed_samples <- c(removed_samples, to_remove)
            }
            
          } else if (biological_strategy == "priority_selection") {
            # Priority: Primary > Recurrent > Metastatic
            if ("sample_type" %in% colnames(patient_samples)) {
              primary <- patient_samples[grepl("Primary", patient_samples$sample_type, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
              
              if (nrow(primary) > 0) {
                patient_samples <- primary
              } else {
                recurrent <- patient_samples[grepl("Recurrent", patient_samples$sample_type, ignore.case = TRUE), ]
                if (nrow(recurrent) > 0) {
                  patient_samples <- recurrent
                }
              }
            }
            
            # If still multiple, select first
            if (nrow(patient_samples) > 1) {
              # Select sample with most genes detected
              common_samples <- intersect(rownames(patient_samples), colnames(expression_data))
              if (length(common_samples) > 1) {
                genes_detected <- colSums(expression_data[, common_samples, drop = FALSE] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
                best_sample <- names(which.max(genes_detected))
                patient_samples <- patient_samples[best_sample, , drop = FALSE]
              } else {
                patient_samples <- patient_samples[1, , drop = FALSE]
              }
            }
            
            keep_sample <- rownames(patient_samples)
            to_remove <- setdiff(sample_ids, keep_sample)
            selected_samples <- setdiff(selected_samples, to_remove)
            removed_samples <- c(removed_samples, to_remove)
            
            for (sample in to_remove) {
              handling_log <- rbind(handling_log, data.frame(
                sample_id = sample,
                action = "removed",
                reason = sprintf("Kept %s based on priority", keep_sample),
                stringsAsFactors = FALSE
              ))
            }
            
          } else if (biological_strategy == "keep_separate") {
            # Keep all biological replicates
            cat(sprintf("  Keeping all %d samples for patient %s\n", 
                        length(sample_ids), patient_id))
          }
        }
      }
      
      cat(sprintf("  Processed %d patients with biological replicates\n", length(patient_groups)))
    }
  }
  
  # Final summary
  cat("\n" )
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("  DUPLICATE HANDLING SUMMARY                                  \n")
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
  cat(sprintf("Original samples: %d\n", nrow(clinical_data)))
  cat(sprintf("Final samples: %d\n", length(selected_samples)))
  cat(sprintf("Removed/averaged: %d\n", length(removed_samples)))
  cat(sprintf("Unique patients: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$patient_id[clinical_data$patient_id %in% clinical_data[selected_samples, "patient_id"]]))))
  cat("\n")
  
  # Return processed data
  return(list(
    clinical = clinical_data[selected_samples, ],
    expression = expression_data[, selected_samples],
    handling_log = handling_log,
    removed_samples = removed_samples,
    strategy_used = list(
      biological = biological_strategy,
      technical = technical_strategy
    )
  ))
}

# ========================================================================
# Continue with rest of the pipeline (QC, modeling, etc.)
# ========================================================================

# [Rest of the pipeline functions remain the same as before]
# prepare_cpsm_format, perform_quality_control, discover_survival_genes,
# train_survival_models, validate_biomarkers,#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
# ========================================================================
# COMPLETE GBM (GLIOBLASTOMA) CPSM ANALYSIS PIPELINE
# ========================================================================
# Advanced pipeline for glioblastoma survival prediction using
# Clinical Prognostic Simulation Method (CPSM) with sophisticated 
# duplicate handling for TCGA data
#
# Features:
# - TCGA data download with multiple fallback strategies
# - Advanced duplicate detection (biological vs technical)
# - Correlation-based technical replicate identification
# - Multiple survival modeling approaches
# - Comprehensive biomarker validation
# - Automated report generation
# ========================================================================

# Load required libraries
cat("Loading required libraries...\n")
required_packages <- c(
  "TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt",
  "survival", "survminer", "randomForestSRC", 
  "glmnet", "caret", "dplyr", "ggplot2"
)

# Install missing packages
for (pkg in required_packages) {
  if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE)) {
    cat(sprintf("Installing %s...\n", pkg))
    if (pkg %in% c("TCGAbiolinks", "SummarizedExperiment", "biomaRt")) {
      if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
      BiocManager::install(pkg, ask = FALSE)
    } else {
      install.packages(pkg)
    }
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }
}

cat("✓ All packages loaded successfully\n\n")

# ========================================================================
# SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS & DIAGNOSTICS
# ========================================================================

check_system_requirements <- function() {
  cat("SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS CHECK\n")
  cat("========================\n")
  
  issues <- 0
  
  # Check R version
  cat(sprintf("✓ R Version: %s\n", R.version.string))
  
  # Check packages
  for (pkg in required_packages) {
    if (requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      cat(sprintf("✓ %s: %s\n", pkg, packageVersion(pkg)))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("❌ %s: NOT INSTALLED\n", pkg))
      issues <- issues + 1
    }
  }
  
  # Test internet connectivity
  cat("\nTesting TCGA API connectivity...\n")
  tryCatch({
    con <- url("https://api.gdc.cancer.gov/status", "r")
    readLines(con, n = 1)
    close(con)
    cat("✓ TCGA API: Accessible\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("❌ TCGA API: Not accessible\n")
    issues <- issues + 1
  })
  
  # Check disk space
  cat("\nSystem resources:\n")
  if (.Platform$OS.type == "unix") {
    tryCatch({
      disk_info <- system("df -h .", intern = TRUE)
      cat(paste(disk_info[1:2], collapse = "\n"))
    }, error = function(e) cat("Could not check disk space\n"))
  }
  
  cat("\n", rep("=", 40), "\n")
  if (issues == 0) {
    cat("✅ All checks passed - Ready for analysis!\n")
    return(TRUE)
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("❌ Found %d issues - Please resolve before proceeding\n", issues))
    return(FALSE)
  }
}

# ========================================================================
# TCGA DATA DOWNLOAD WITH ROBUST ERROR HANDLING
# ========================================================================

download_tcga_gbm_data <- function() {
  cat("DOWNLOADING TCGA-GBM DATA\n")
  cat("========================\n")

  # NEW: check local cache first
  cat("Checking for local pre-downloaded GDC data...\n")
  local_data <- load_local_gdc_gbm(verbose = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(local_data)) {
    cat("✓ Using local data — skipping download.\n\n")
    return(local_data)
  }
  cat("Local data not available. Proceeding to download strategies.\n\n")
  
  strategies <- list(
    # Strategy 1: Standard API download
    function() {
      cat("Strategy 1: Standard API download...\n")
      query <- GDCquery(
        project = "TCGA-GBM",
        data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling", 
        data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification",
        workflow.type = "STAR - Counts",
        sample.type = "Primary Tumor"
      )
      GDCdownload(query, method = "api", files.per.chunk = 10)
      GDCprepare(query, save = TRUE, save.filename = "TCGA_GBM_data.rda")
    },
    
    # Strategy 2: Smaller chunks
    function() {
      cat("Strategy 2: Small chunk download...\n")
      query <- GDCquery(
        project = "TCGA-GBM",
        data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
        data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification", 
        workflow.type = "STAR - Counts"
      )
      GDCdownload(query, method = "api", files.per.chunk = 3)
      GDCprepare(query)
    },
    
    # Strategy 3: Check for cached data
    function() {
      cat("Strategy 3: Looking for cached data...\n")
      cache_files <- list.files(".", pattern = "TCGA.*GBM.*\\.rda", full.names = TRUE)
      if (length(cache_files) > 0) {
        load(cache_files[1])
        if (exists("data")) return(data)
      }
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  # Try each strategy
  for (i in seq_along(strategies)) {
    cat(sprintf("\n--- Attempting Strategy %d ---\n", i))
    tryCatch({
      result <- strategies[[i]]()
      if (!is.null(result)) {
        cat(sprintf("✅ Success with Strategy %d!\n", i))
        
        # Validate downloaded data
        if (is(result, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
          n_samples <- ncol(result)
          n_genes <- nrow(result)
          cat(sprintf("Data validated: %d samples, %d genes\n", n_samples, n_genes))
          return(result)
        }
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("❌ Strategy %d failed: %s\n", i, e$message))
    })
  }
  
  cat("\n❌ All download strategies failed\n")
  cat("Troubleshooting options:\n")
  cat("1. Check internet connection\n")
  cat("2. Try again later (TCGA servers may be busy)\n") 
  cat("3. Use simulated data for testing\n")
  
  return(NULL)
}

# ========================================================================
# ADVANCED DUPLICATE DETECTION & ANALYSIS
# ========================================================================

analyze_tcga_duplicates <- function(se_data) {
  cat("COMPREHENSIVE DUPLICATE ANALYSIS\n")
  cat("===============================\n")
  
  clinical_data <- as.data.frame(colData(se_data))
  expression_data <- assay(se_data, "fpkm_unstrand")
  
  # Extract TCGA barcode components
  if ("submitter_id" %in% colnames(clinical_data)) {
    clinical_data$patient_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 12)
    clinical_data$sample_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 16) 
    clinical_data$portion_id <- substr(clinical_data$submitter_id, 1, 19)
    clinical_data$aliquot_id <- clinical_data$submitter_id
  } else {
    stop("Cannot extract TCGA barcode structure")
  }
  
  # Analyze duplicate patterns
  patient_counts <- table(clinical_data$patient_id)
  sample_counts <- table(clinical_data$sample_id)
  
  cat(sprintf("Total samples: %d\n", nrow(clinical_data)))
  cat(sprintf("Unique patients: %d\n", length(unique(clinical_data$patient_id))))
  cat(sprintf("Patients with multiple samples: %d\n", sum(patient_counts > 1)))
  
  # Identify duplicate types
  duplicates <- list(
    biological = clinical_data[clinical_data$patient_id %in% names(patient_counts[patient_counts > 1]), ],
    technical = clinical_data[clinical_data$sample_id %in% names(sample_counts[sample_counts > 1]), ]
  )
  
  # Perform correlation analysis for technical replicates
  correlations <- analyze_sample_correlations(duplicates, expression_data)
  
  # Select optimal samples
  selected_samples <- select_optimal_samples(clinical_data, expression_data, duplicates, correlations)
  
  return(selected_samples)
}

analyze_sample_correlations <- function(duplicates, expression_data) {
  cat("Analyzing sample correlations...\n")
  
  correlations <- list()
  
  # Function to calculate pairwise correlations
  calc_correlations <- function(sample_ids) {
    if (length(sample_ids) < 2) return(NULL)
    
    common_samples <- intersect(sample_ids, colnames(expression_data))
    if (length(common_samples) < 2) return(NULL)
    
    expr_subset <- expression_data[, common_samples]
    
    # Remove zero-variance genes
    gene_vars <- apply(expr_subset, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
    expr_subset <- expr_subset[gene_vars > 0, ]
    
    # Calculate correlation matrix
    cor_matrix <- cor(expr_subset, use = "complete.obs")
    
    # Extract pairwise correlations
    n <- ncol(cor_matrix)
    pairs <- expand.grid(1:n, 1:n)
    pairs <- pairs[pairs$Var1 < pairs$Var2, ]
    
    data.frame(
      sample1 = colnames(cor_matrix)[pairs$Var1],
      sample2 = colnames(cor_matrix)[pairs$Var2], 
      correlation = cor_matrix[cbind(pairs$Var1, pairs$Var2)]
    )
  }
  
  # Analyze biological duplicates
  if (nrow(duplicates$biological) > 0) {
    bio_groups <- split(rownames(duplicates$biological), duplicates$biological$patient_id)
    bio_cors <- do.call(rbind, lapply(bio_groups, calc_correlations))
    
    if (!is.null(bio_cors) && nrow(bio_cors) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("Biological duplicate correlations: mean=%.3f, range=%.3f-%.3f\n",
                  mean(bio_cors$correlation), min(bio_cors$correlation), max(bio_cors$correlation)))
      correlations$biological <- bio_cors
    }
  }
  
  # Analyze technical replicates  
  if (nrow(duplicates$technical) > 0) {
    tech_groups <- split(rownames(duplicates$technical), duplicates$technical$sample_id)
    tech_cors <- do.call(rbind, lapply(tech_groups, calc_correlations))
    
    if (!is.null(tech_cors) && nrow(tech_cors) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("Technical replicate correlations: mean=%.3f, range=%.3f-%.3f\n",
                  mean(tech_cors$correlation), min(tech_cors$correlation), max(tech_cors$correlation)))
      
      # Flag potential issues
      high_cor_tech <- tech_cors[tech_cors$correlation > 0.95, ]
      if (nrow(high_cor_tech) > 0) {
        cat(sprintf("Found %d high-correlation technical replicate pairs\n", nrow(high_cor_tech)))
      }
      
      correlations$technical <- tech_cors
    }
  }
  
  return(correlations)
}

select_optimal_samples <- function(clinical_data, expression_data, duplicates, correlations) {
  cat("Selecting optimal samples...\n")
  
  selected_samples <- rownames(clinical_data)
  removed_samples <- character(0)
  
  # Remove high-correlation technical replicates first
  if ("technical" %in% names(correlations)) {
    high_cor_pairs <- correlations$technical[correlations$technical$correlation > 0.95, ]
    
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(high_cor_pairs))) {
      pair <- high_cor_pairs[i, ]
      if (pair$sample1 %in% selected_samples && pair$sample2 %in% selected_samples) {
        # Remove the lexicographically later sample for reproducibility
        to_remove <- max(pair$sample1, pair$sample2)
        selected_samples <- setdiff(selected_samples, to_remove)
        removed_samples <- c(removed_samples, to_remove)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Handle biological duplicates - prefer primary tumors
```
